programme.title: My Cat from Hell

programme.title: Dr Jeff: Rocky Mountain Vet
programme.desc: A beloved poodle is rushed into surgery, Dr Jeff heads out to operate on an alligator, and four turtles, unable to eat, get their first vet visit in 43 years

programme.title: Pit Bulls and Parolees
programme.desc: Tragedy strikes after Tia and M2 agree to take two emaciated hounds from a shelter. Mariah and Lizzy help two dogs while working through the chaos of the pandemic

programme.title: My Cat from Hell
programme.desc: Behaviourist Jackson Galaxy helps two women whose friendship is being put to the test by a Bengal cat. Plus, a feline with a nasty streak causes concern for a mother-to-be

programme.title: My Cat from Hell
programme.desc: Jackson Galaxy assists a couple whose violent cats are stopping them from moving in together, and a war veteran whose feline reacts violently to his girlfriends tiny dog

programme.title: Treehouse Masters
programme.desc: A couple dreams of a treehouse that will recapture mountain vistas, so Pete Nelson creates a bungalow with large windows to take in a view that was once lost

programme.title: Meet the Orangutans
programme.desc: The lives of orphaned baby orangutans in the worlds oldest sanctuary in Borneo. Toddlers are wheelbarrowed in for lessons in life

programme.title: Meet the Orangutans
programme.desc: One orphan is new on the scene and needs a more experienced friend to help them bed in. Meanwhile, the babies are at high risk from ingested parasites

programme.title: How Do Animals Do That?
programme.desc: How do dogs tell time? What is the secret behind the kangaroos hop? Plus, why do vultures fly in circles? Experts answer these questions!

programme.title: How Do Animals Do That?
programme.desc: Amazing animal facts, including how cats always land on their feet, hummingbirds ability to fly when it is raining and a subterranean mammals secret to longevity

programme.title: The Diamond Collar
programme.desc: James Giuliani and Lena Perrelli find a place to keep their rescue animals, but face plenty of work before it can be opened and made ready for its new occupants

programme.title: The Diamond Collar

programme.title: Alaska Gold Diggers
programme.desc: SJ heads to Fairbanks, as he entrusts John and his men to continue the Gold Run operation. Meanwhile, already in the city, the women are met with a new set of challenges

programme.title: Alaska: Homestead Rescue
programme.desc: The Raneys head off to answer an SOS call from an off-grid couple. but with severe storms threatening floods, they are in a race against Mother Nature

programme.title: Air Jaws
programme.desc: Famed shark photographer Chris Fallows embarks on his eighth Air Jaws adventure, setting out to discover how great white sharks manage to hunt in total darkness

programme.title: The Haunted

programme.title: Tiger Shark King
programme.desc: Documentary examining the secret lives of tiger sharks, one of the most deadly predators in the oceans

programme.title: How Do Animals Do That?
programme.desc: How can elephants hear something hundreds of miles away? What goes on inside a chickens egg? How has an incredible snake learnt how to fly?

programme.title: The Good Old Days
programme.desc: Leonard Sachs chairs the old-time music hall programme, inviting Bernard Cribbins, Sandie Shaw, Bill McCue and Francis Van Dyke to entertain an audience at the Leeds City Varieties. First shown in April 1973

programme.title: Hetty Wainthropp Investigates
programme.desc: The sleuth tries to help when a woman distracted by her mobile phone inadvertently causes a car crash and the other driver is so overcome by road rage she assaults her. Hetty tries to fix the missing pieces of the puzzle into place and uncovers a family tragedy

programme.title: Dusty Springfield at the BBC
programme.desc: A selection of archive appearances on the BBC by singer Dusty Springfield through the decades, including performances of You Dont Have to Say You Love Me, Son of a Preacher Man and The Look of Love.

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Jamie Theakston presents the pop chart programme, first broadcast on September 24, 1999. Featuring Vengaboys, Sting, Everything but the Girl, Paul Johnson, Wyclef Jean and Bono, David Bowie, Tom Jones and The Cardigans and Eiffel 65

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Jayne Middlemiss presents a roadshow edition of the pop chart programme, first broadcast on October 1, 1999. Featuring DJ Jean, Shania Twain, Kelle Bryan, David Bowie, S Club 7, Britney Spears, Lou Bega and Eiffel 65.

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Gary Davies and Nicky Campbell introduce the edition from April 24, featuring Pat and Mick, James Brown, Fairground Attraction, Brenda Russell, the Primitives, Luther Vandross, Joyce Sims, Bananarama, Will Downing, SExpress and Scott Fitzgerald

programme.title: The Good Old Days
programme.desc: Leonard Sachs chairs the old-time music hall programme, inviting Bernard Cribbins, Sandie Shaw, Bill McCue and Francis Van Dyke to entertain an audience at the Leeds City Varieties. First shown in April 1973

programme.title: Wildlife on Two
programme.sub-title: A Wonderful Bird Is the Pelican
programme.desc: David Attenborough narrates a profile of one of natures most unusual birds, with the large water bird noted for its huge pouch and clumsy, waddling gait. First broadcast in 1997

programme.title: Hetty Wainthropp Investigates
programme.desc: The sleuth tries to help when a woman distracted by her mobile phone inadvertently causes a car crash and the other driver is so overcome by road rage she assaults her. Hetty tries to fix the missing pieces of the puzzle into place and uncovers a family tragedy

programme.title: The Count of Monte Cristo
programme.desc: Betrayed on his wedding day, young French sailor Edmond Dantès is falsely accused of treason and condemned to years of brutal imprisonment in the Château dIf, where an unexpected encounter plants the seed of revenge. Adaptation of Alexandre Dumas adventure, starring Pierre Niney. In French

programme.title: The Count of Monte Cristo
programme.desc: Escaping prison and uncovering a fabled fortune, Edmond reinvents himself as the mysterious Count of Monte Cristo and returns to a world that has prospered without him. Adaptation of Alexandre Dumas adventure, starring Pierre Niney. In French

programme.title: Keeping Up Appearances
programme.desc: Hyacinth decides that she and husband Richard should have a weekend at a golfing hotel so that she can keep up with the Major and his wife. Roy Clarkes suburban sitcom, starring Patricia Routledge and Clive Swift

programme.title: Sorry!
programme.desc: Timothy attempts to put his dreams into practice as the annual promotion interview looms - by taking up sleepwalking. Ronnie Corbett stars

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: A retrospective look at TV appearances made over the years by award-winning actor Albert Finney, with interviews from the archive and classic clips capturing the milestones and highlights of his life and career. Narrated by Sylvia Syms

programme.title: Genius of the Ancient World
programme.desc: Bettany Hughes series exploring the lives and teachings of influential ancient philosophers draws to a close with a profile of Confucius, who is credited as the first Chinese thinker to take a systematic philosophical approach to issues of social, political and ethical importance. Born during the chaotic Spring and Autumn period of Chinese history, Confucius believed that harmony could be promoted and achieved through the examples of sage rulers. He was also a innovative teacher, and tried to instill his principles of moral excellence in Chinas elite, but died without seeing his vision for his nation come to pass. However, his philosophies would eventually form the bedrock of Chinese culture, with many of his principles of tradition surviving to this day

programme.title: Wildlife on Two
programme.sub-title: A Wonderful Bird Is the Pelican
programme.desc: David Attenborough narrates a profile of one of natures most unusual birds, with the large water bird noted for its huge pouch and clumsy, waddling gait. First broadcast in 1997

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Sun
programme.desc: Using satellite images and the expertise of Britains leading solar scientists, Kate Humble and Helen Czerski investigate the inner workings of the star and the influence its cycles of activity have on Earth. They reveal the sun is more active now than it has been for a decade, sending out super-heated plasma and waves of radiation, and examine why some experts think longer-term changes in its behaviour may have powerful effects on the worlds climate

programme.title: Travels with Pevsner
programme.desc: Michael Bracewell explores the architecture of Surrey, visiting two masterpieces by Edwin Lutyens and the modernistic heart of Gatwick airport the Beehive

programme.title: Inside Classical
programme.desc: Conductor Ben Gernon joins the BBC Philharmonic Orchestra, with Jennifer Pike in a performance of Bruchs Violin Concerto No 1 and Dvoráks New World Symphony. Dvoráks piece takes inspiration from both Native American and African American melodies. The performance is held in Salford

programme.title: Attenboroughs Passion Projects
programme.desc: David Attenborough reflects on Lost Worlds, Vanished Lives, his 1989 series that explored the worlds major fossil sites and looked at how the organic remains fire peoples imaginations and teach not just about the lives of long-extinct animals, but also the world they lived in. First broadcast in 2016

programme.title: Darcey Bussell on The Magic of Dance
programme.desc: The former ballerina reflects on Margot Fonteyns 1979 series, picking her favourite moments from a programme that included a tap masterclass with Sammy Davis Jr, a routine by ballet star Mikhail Baryshnikov and a glimpse of modern dance pioneer Isadora Duncan

programme.title: The Magic of Dance with Margot Fonteyn
programme.desc: The former ballerina explores the world of dance, beginning by following the dominance of the ballerina in the 1930s to the emergence of the great male dancers of the 1960s and 1970s. Her story is illustrated by some of the worlds greatest dancers performing some of the eras most enduring dances, including Rudolk Nureyev, Fred Astaire and Sammy Davis Jr. First broadcast in 1979

programme.title: Sammy Davis Jr at the BBC
programme.desc: A collection of the singer, actor and dancers finest moments on British TV, showing off his unbeatable talents as an entertainer and opening up about his life in several fascinating conversations with the likes of Michael Parkinson and Terry Wogan. He discusses his encounters with racism and his relationship with Americas civil rights movement, the breakdown of his marriage to actress May Britt, and the car accident that cost him an eye. Alongside the insights into his life and experiences are archive performances of Once In a Lifetime, Me and My Shadow and My Funny Valentine

programme.title: Art That Made Us
programme.desc: The story of Britain in the 18th century, which saw an explosion of creativity and a country with enough money, from trade and conquest, to pay for it. Sculptor Thomas J Price visits Harewood House to see the elaborate Robert Adam-designed interiors, Joshua Reynolds portraits and Thomas Chippendale furniture that the slave trade paid for. This was also the great age of mockery, and artist Lubaina Himid reflects on William Hogarths scabrous exposure of upper-class hypocrisies, while Stewart Lee analyses the cutting humour of A Modest Proposal, Jonathan Swifts bitter satire about the treatment of the Irish poor, with passages performed by Jason Isaacs

programme.title: Travels with Pevsner
programme.desc: Michael Bracewell explores the architecture of Surrey, visiting two masterpieces by Edwin Lutyens and the modernistic heart of Gatwick airport the Beehive

programme.title: Inside Classical
programme.desc: Conductor Ben Gernon joins the BBC Philharmonic Orchestra, with Jennifer Pike in a performance of Bruchs Violin Concerto No 1 and Dvoráks New World Symphony. Dvoráks piece takes inspiration from both Native American and African American melodies. The performance is held in Salford

programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by chefs Vivek Singh and Donna Hay, and special guest Lucy Beaumont. Olly Smith picks the wines to go with the studio dishes

programme.title: Flaming Feasts
programme.desc: Chris Roberts explores the food scene of Arberth in Pembrokeshire, enjoying Spanish tapas at Ultracomida, a Catalan-inspired deli and tapas bar that transformed the towns food culture. He also samples vermouth crafted from foraged Welsh botanicals and Dexter beef from cattle on the Preseli Mountains before foraging for a fire-cooked feast

programme.desc: A marathon runner completes a seven-year project to transform a site in Llanfynydd into smart holiday accommodation while a couple give a terraced house in Derby the wow factor. Friends in Southend-on-Sea create a lovely coastal home

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Charlie Ross presents the show from Oswestry in Shropshire, and visits a museum of model cars. Experts Charles Hanson and Ben Cooper assist the teams in finding a bargain

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Hazel Irvine presents live coverage of the afternoon session on day 15 at the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield, featuring the fourth and final session of the first semi-final. There was no play at this stage last year, as Zhao Xintong had already completed a 17-7 victory over Ronnie OSullivan. With commentary and analysis from Steve Davis, Ken Doherty, Steven Hallworth, Stephen Hendry, John Parrott, Joe Perry, Dennis Taylor and Lee Walker

programme.title: Final Score

programme.title: Celebrity Bridge of Lies
programme.desc: Ross Kemp presents the high stakes celebrity quiz. Comedian and actor Phil Wang, dating expert Anna Williamson, actor and comedian Miles Jupp, and actor Jamie-Lee ODonnell must cross the Bridge by stepping on the truths and avoiding the lies, earning money for the charity of their choice with each correct answer

programme.title: Blankety Blank
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh hosts the comedy quiz show in which a panel of six celebrities fill in the missing blanks and help win some brilliant prizes for some very lucky contestants. This week, he is joined by Joe Marler, Sue Perkins, Trevor Nelson, Josh Pugh, Tasha Ghouri and Sara Barron

programme.title: Michael McIntyres Big Show
programme.desc: Michael presents another hour of entertainment, featuring music from Olly Murs, singing Dancing on Cars, and Stacey Dooley handing over her mobile in Send to All. Theres a rude awakening for Alexander Armstrong when he plays the Midnight Gameshow and a pair of Unexpected Stars of the Show are invited to perform a duet

programme.title: The Weakest Link
programme.desc: Romesh Ranganathan is joined by a line-up of TV medics in this edition of the celebrity quiz. Dr Ranj Singh, Kirsty Mitchell, Dr Xand van Tulleken, Dr Amir Khan, Renee Bailey, Rosie Marcel, Wendi Peters and Dr Linda Papadopoulos answer general knowledge questions against the clock and try to avoid being voted off as they play to win a cash prize for the charity of their choice

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: The paramedics face a life-or-death situation in the call centre, as a grieving father is consumed with anger and goes looking for someone to blame for his loss. Flynn confirms that the supposed viral outbreak is an unknown toxin, and Cam feels he has nothing to contribute, until he makes a big difference in the lives of two dementia patients

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: Lee and Lucy look to a career in the movies when they play background elves on the set of a popular TV mini-series, Dragon Castle. Their aspirations to become leading actors, however, are thwarted by their lack of talent

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Arsenal v Fulham at Emirates Stadium and Brentford v West Ham United at Gtech Community Stadium. Arsenals title challenge continued against a side they overcame 1-0 at Craven Cottage in October. Brentford are bidding to qualify for Europe, while the Hammers were also in need of points to boost their chances of avoiding relegation. Plus, Bournemouth v Crystal Palace, Newcastle United v Brighton and Hove Albion and Wolverhampton Wanderers v Sunderland

programme.title: The Football Interview
programme.desc: Kelly Somers has a conversation with Nottingham Forest and England midfielder Elliott Anderson

programme.title: Point Break (1991)
programme.desc: An FBI agent infiltrates a Californian surfing gang suspected of carrying out a series of armed robberies wearing masks modelled on former US presidents. However, his loyalties are tested to the limits when he is befriended by the gangs leader. Kathryn Bigelows thriller, starring Keanu Reeves, Patrick Swayze, Gary Busey and Lori Petty

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Its a family affair this week as Steve Fletcher fixes a model watermill, while his son Fred brings a cherished clock back to life - with Steves sister, leather expert Suzie, watching their progress from behind her own bench. Meanwhile, Rebecca Bissonnet works on a damaged headpiece worn in the Notting Hill Carnival and master cobbler Dean Westmoreland sets to work on a pair of vintage speed skates belonging to Steve Humber, who went on to become a member of the British Olympic team

programme.title: Trolls World Tour (2020)
programme.desc: Poppy and Branch discover there are different troll tribes scattered over six different lands. Each tribe is also devoted to different kinds of music - funk, country, techno, classical, pop and rock. When rockers Queen Barb and King Thrash decide to destroy the other music, Poppy and Branch embark on a mission to unite the trolls and save the diverse melodies from becoming extinct. Animated adventure, with the voices of Anna Kendrick, Justin Timberlake and Rachel Bloom

programme.title: Points of View
programme.desc: Viewers opinions on BBC shows and behind-the-scenes reports on new programmes

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Hazel Irvine presents live coverage of the first eight frames of the final on day 16 at the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield. Zhao Xintong took early control of last years final, taking a 7-1 lead over Mark Williams. With commentary and analysis from Steve Davis, Ken Doherty, Steven Hallworth, Stephen Hendry, John Parrott, Joe Perry, Dennis Taylor and Lee Walker

programme.title: Secret Garden
programme.desc: David Attenborough reveals the animals that have made a home in a woodland garden hidden at the bottom of a steep-sided Welsh valley. A tawny owl hunts for rodents drawn to the garden bird feeders, while a dormouse hibernates beneath the fallen leaves. In spring, a queen buff-tailed bumblebee emerges from a winter underground and prepares to build a new colony and a badger mother brings her young above ground for the first time

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Weather
programme.desc: The latest forecast

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Matt Baker and Vick Hope explore the chalk landscapes of the Chilterns, where rare grasslands, seasonal streams and specialised wildlife all depend on the fragile ground. Matt meets farmers working together to reconnect these landscapes, while Vick visits a rare winterbourne stream that flows only after winter rains. Plus, Adam Henson welcomes a rare breed Middle White pig to his Cotswolds farm

programme.title: Secret Garden
programme.desc: David Attenborough reveals how a remote garden on Scotlands west coast has become an oasis for barn owls and pine martens as they battle the elements. During the brief summer, crossbills pry seeds from the cones in specially planted Scots pine trees, house martins raise their chicks, reptiles are finally warm enough to breed, and carnivorous sundews trap insects in a specially created bog garden

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce foxtrots with Anton Du Beke and explores a collection of clocks in the grounds of Belmont House in Kent. Treasures include a piece of HMS Bounty, a pair of bronze owls with some surprising contents, and a silver honey pot. Plus, Runjeet Singh explores newly discovered photographic archive revealing the story of Sikh worship in this corner of Kent

programme.title: Making Life on Earth: Attenboroughs Greatest Adventure
programme.desc: The making of the acclaimed wildlife series that took three years to film, featuring 40 countries and over 600 species. First broadcast in 1979 and watched by 500 million people, it launched David Attenboroughs career as arguably the most successful and influential wildlife film-maker of all time

programme.title: The Cage
programme.desc: Leanne and Matty panic as Nancy and Gary review the casino books. As danger grows, Ning urges Matty to spy while Gary closes in on Leanne

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Action from the latest matches in the Premier League, including Manchester United v Liverpool at Old Trafford and Aston Villa v Tottenham Hotspur at Villa Park. United were looking to do the double over their fierce rivals and further enhance their chances of finishing above them in the table. The Villans were aiming to move a step closer to securing Champions League football as they took on relegation-threatened Spurs. Plus, Bournemouth v Crystal Place and a round-up of Saturdays goals

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Two Pints of Lager and a Packet of Crisps
programme.desc: Jonny makes a startling discovery about Corinthians parentage, while Janet and Donna argue when they both become convinced they will get the job managing the Archer. Sitcom, starring Ralf Little, Natalie Casey and Sheridan Smith

programme.title: Two Pints of Lager and a Packet of Crisps
programme.desc: Janet starts her job managing the Archer and, in a bid to attract an older clientele, decides to give the pub a wartime theme. Donna is still intent on revenge, Jonny helps Gaz become a dad and Louise turns to prostitution. Comedy, starring Sheridan Smith and Natalie Casey

programme.title: The Beauty Queen and the Catfish
programme.desc: Abbie Draper takes her findings to the police, but they are unable to do anything about it and the Catfish of Kilmarnock remains free to target more and more victims. However, Abbie cannot walk away and steps up her investigation over months that turn into years, with victims spread across the country and dating back over a decade

programme.title: The Beauty Queen and the Catfish
programme.desc: The victims finally have justice, but are still left urgently searching for answers. What drives someone, with a seemingly normal life to terrorise countless women from the same town? Those closest to the perpetrator, who are also trying to understand the mystery behind the repeated cycles of offending, share their thoughts

programme.title: Stacey Dooley
programme.desc: Invited into the traditionally private gypsy community, Stacey discovers the complex balancing act young women face growing up in one of Britains most maligned ethnic minorities. In Bedfordshire she meets 23-year-old Chantelle, who prides herself in keeping with the old gypsy values her grandmother taught her and shares them on social media, where she has nearly 400,000 TikTok followers. In Lincolnshire, the reporter meets 18-year-old Serena, who is planning her wedding to her fiance Marco. He is also from a gypsy and traveller community and Serena wouldnt have it any other way

programme.title: BBC Comedy Short Films
programme.desc: When a teenage boy has a girl over for the first time, the overly attached monster under his bed has a meltdown. Comedy short film, starring Freddie Meredith

programme.title: BBC Comedy Short Films
programme.desc: Short film about four very different schoolgirls thrown together as they plan their escape from detention. Written by and starring Sabrina Ali

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: The newly regenerated 12th Doctor arrives in Victorian London to find a dinosaur causing havoc in the Thames. Reunited with old friends Vastra, Jenny and Strax, the Doctor investigates a spate of deadly spontaneous combustions, while Clara struggles with whether or not this darker new incarnation can possibly be the man she knew. Peter Capaldi makes his debut as the Doctor in an episode directed by Ben Wheatley (High-Rise, Sightseers)

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: A film crew comes to Button House to shoot a reconstruction of an assassination plot for a historical documentary - a scheme which may have involved one of the spirits. Mike persuades Alison to give an interview about the old house in the hope of drumming up some publicity, forcing her to turn to Thomas for advice on the art of public speaking

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: A mysterious visitor arrives at Button House, with a revelation that leaves Alison reeling. Thomas is confused by his feelings for this strange arrival, while Lady Button and Julian warn of sinister motives, and Kitty is as territorial as ever. Mike has an epiphany on his first day at a new job, which triggers Julian to remember his first day as a ghost

programme.title: Miranda
programme.desc: Prompted by pressure from her mother, Miranda tries to improve her career prospects, but her decision to become a waitress embarrasses Penny and threatens to turn her old school friend Tillys promotion celebrations into a fiasco. Garys return from an RAF reunion while still in uniform adds to the confusion. Comedy, starring Miranda Hart, Patricia Hodge and Sally Phillips

programme.title: Miranda
programme.desc: In a bid to make the most of being young, free and single, Miranda spontaneously books herself into a hotel across the road. She befriends helpful porter Jason and conference organiser Colin, and by the end of her stay, she feels like a new woman. Starring Miranda Hart and Luke Pasqualino

programme.title: Quiz Lady (2023)
programme.desc: Mild-mannered accounting services worker Anne Yum receives unsettling news that her mother has disappeared from her care home. Moreover, the missing matriarch has spent $80,000 feeding a gambling habit and an angry creditor is holding Annes beloved dog hostage as collateral. Annes extrovert sister Jenny conceives a perfect plan to raise the cash involving playing for the jackpot on her favourite TV show. The siblings embark on a madcap cross-country odyssey to transform shrinking violet Anne into a game show contender and defeat its slippery reigning champion. Comedy, starring Sandra Oh, Will Ferrell and Awkwafina

programme.title: Paranormal: Britains Last Witch
programme.desc: Disturbed by a recording of what appears to be a spirit voice and accounts of the late medium Helen Duncan summoning physical spirit forms, Sian Eleri focuses on evidence from those who believe this apparent seance to be fake. Searching for answers and hoping to understand how Helen was convincing people they were communicating with their dead loved ones, Sian tracks down a modern-day medium. He claims to commune with the other side, but a seance in Edinburgh leaves Sian with more questions than she first started with

programme.title: Paranormal: Britains Last Witch
programme.desc: Sian finds a medium in the Netherlands who claims they bring back spirits in physical form. This could be a glimpse into Helens world, but Sians been warned there are dangers. Back in the UK, Sian reads an astonishing account of Helen bringing back a Second World War sailor from the dead in Portsmouth

programme.title: Two Pints of Lager and a Packet of Crisps
programme.desc: The gang struggles with problems as Janet tries to find a way to keep the pub open, and Louise feels unwell - though Kelly thinks she recognises the symptoms. Jonny worries he has fallen in love with Gaz and the arrival of Donnas brother causes a stir. Sheridan Smith and Kathryn Drysdale star

programme.title: Two Pints of Lager and a Packet of Crisps
programme.desc: Horror-themed episode of the comedy in which the gang learns the Archer is facing demolition and breaks in the night before to bid the place farewell. However, the friends soon find themselves trapped inside the sinister pub - where strange forces are intent on killing them. Ralf Little stars

programme.title: Two Pints of Lager and a Packet of Crisps
programme.desc: Jonny wins a competition to jump over a shark in Hawaii, leading Gaz to try to prove that he is more exciting than his mate. Meanwhile, the girls compete for a job behind the bar at the newly refurbished Archer pub. Sheridan Smith, Natalie Casey, Will Mellor and Kathryn Drysdale star

programme.title: Two Pints of Lager and a Packet of Crisps
programme.desc: Janet struggles to accept Jonnys death, and decides she must race through the stages of grief - denial, anger, bargaining, depression and acceptance - for the sake of her son Corinthian. Louise struggles to convince her friends she is pregnant while keeping her Auntie Val in the dark. Comedy, starring Sheridan Smith

programme.title: Miranda
programme.desc: Prompted by pressure from her mother, Miranda tries to improve her career prospects, but her decision to become a waitress embarrasses Penny and threatens to turn her old school friend Tillys promotion celebrations into a fiasco. Garys return from an RAF reunion while still in uniform adds to the confusion. Comedy, starring Miranda Hart, Patricia Hodge and Sally Phillips

programme.title: Miranda
programme.desc: In a bid to make the most of being young, free and single, Miranda spontaneously books herself into a hotel across the road. programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Max attempts to court Cindy, Walford welcomes a surprise visitor, and the Gulatis and Panesars try to support Ravi

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: The Mitchells try to get through to Phil, Cindy and Max have a close call, and the Slaters surprise Big Mo

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Phils search continues, two Walford residents face a race against time, and Nigel receives some visitors

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Albert Square comes together to celebrate a friend, Phil battles his inner demons, while Max and Cindys romance causes friction

programme.title: Leigh-Anne: Race, Pop and Power
programme.desc: Documentary following pop star Leigh-Anne Pinnock as she confronts her experience as the only black member of Little Mix, and as a black woman in the music industry

programme.title: The Woman in Black (2012)
programme.desc: A lawyer tormented by the death of his wife is sent to a remote village to put deceased client Alice Drablows affairs in order. The locals are suspicious of outsiders and determined to keep the areas tragic history a secret, and the late Mrs Drablows isolated house appears to be haunted by a vengeful spirit that seems hell-bent on redressing a decades-past grievance. Horror based on Susan Hills novel, with Daniel Radcliffe and Ciaran Hinds

programme.title: Paranormal: Britains Last Witch
programme.desc: Sian Eleri recounts the story of Helen Duncan, who claimed she could summon the dead. Helen was a Scottish medium, who in 1944 went on trail at the Old Bailey and became the last person convicted and imprisoned under the Witchcraft Act. Sian visits Helens birthplace, Callander, a small town where her childhood premonitions caused fear in the community

programme.title: Paddy and Molly: Show No Mersey
programme.desc: As pressure builds ahead of her next fight in Los Angeles, Molly visits a wedding venue with partner Ellis. Paddy discovers a serious shoulder injury that could scupper his next bout

programme.title: Paddy and Molly: Show No Mersey
programme.desc: Paddy discusses his mental health and the struggles and pressures he faces, while Molly trains in Liverpool for the last time before her fight in Vegas

programme.title: Paranormal: Britains Last Witch
programme.desc: Sian discovers an account of a spirit revealing secret information during a seance. This new evidence could prove Helens abilities - or that she was a spy. Either way, it seems the authorities wanted her out of the way because she was seen as a danger

programme.title: Leigh-Anne: Race, Pop and Power
programme.desc: Documentary following pop star Leigh-Anne Pinnock as she confronts her experience as the only black member of Little Mix, and as a black woman in the music industry

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: The newly regenerated 12th Doctor arrives in Victorian London to find a dinosaur causing havoc in the Thames. Peter Capaldi makes his debut as the Doctor in an episode directed by Ben Wheatley (High-Rise, Sightseers)

programme.title: Black Box (2021)
programme.desc: Obsessive forensic analyst Mathieu Vasseur is disappointed to be sidelined in the examination of the black box of an airliner which has mysteriously crashed in the Alps. But when his boss goes missing, he is asked to step in, and becomes determines to find out the truth behind the crash. Thriller, starring Pierre Niney and Lou de Laage. In French

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: The first quarter-final sees the weeks six most talented amateurs face off against each other for the first time. They begin with an invention test in which they must come up with pancakes of their own choosing, making the most of the fresh produce on offer. Then Jay Rayner arrives to set them a brief - and all he asks for is a salad, which is easier said than done. Whose bowl will be good enough to see them through to knockout week?

programme.title: Interior Design Masters with Alan Carr
programme.desc: Alan and interiors guru Michelle Ogundehin welcome a new group of wannabe designers to the Brighton Design Studio, where each week they are challenged to transform a selection of vastly different spaces. They begin on the coast in Dorset, where they must transform basic wooden beach huts into stylish spaces which also show off their signature styles - while also taking into account sustainability. To judge their efforts, Michelle is joined by leading designer Jonathan Adler

programme.title: Mastermind: To Think Like a Killer
programme.desc: The story of Dr Ann Burgess, the mastermind behind the development of modern serial killer profiling and solving Americas most infamous true-crime cases. This edition takes a look at when Ann joined a task force with the FBI, where she hunted serial predators. She was thrust into an active investigation involving the assault of over 20 women

programme.title: Newsround

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve Backshall goes in search of puffins and dragonflies

programme.title: Gardeners World
programme.desc: Monty Don is busy this week, as the garden bursts into colour. Woody herbs such as thyme and sage dont thrive in his clay soil, so hes sowing seeds to ensure a steady supply. Carol Klein is creating a modest border devoted to vegetables and weaving it together with flowers, to provide year round colour. Also, Advolly Richmond heads to Fife to uncover the story of the first pink cupped daffodil, bred in the early 1900s and still growing today, thanks to two passionate plantswomen

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Rishi Persad presents live coverage of the morning session on day 15 at the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield, featuring the third and penultimate session of the second semi-final. Mark Williams moved into a 13-11 lead over Judd Trump at this stage last year. With commentary and analysis from Steve Davis, Ken Doherty, Steven Hallworth, Stephen Hendry, John Parrott, Joe Perry, Dennis Taylor and Lee Walker

programme.title: The Best Dishes Ever
programme.desc: Ainsley Harriott introduces a selection of recipes to sate cravings for a takeaway, including fish and chips from James Martin and a pizza recipe from Keith Floyd

programme.title: Weather Watchers with Barra Best
programme.desc: Everybody talks about the weather but nobody does anything about it is a witticism attributed to writer Mark Twain. In the first of a two-part series, the weather presenter and journalist experiences how climate shapes peoples lives and discovers that while they cant do anything about the weather, they can certainly do things with it

programme.title: Live Irish Cup
programme.desc: Dungannon Swifts v Coleraine (Kick-off 2.30pm). Stephen Watson presents all the action from the showpiece match at Windsor Park. Dungannon are the holders of this competition and defeated Clitfonville on penalties to reach another final. Coleraine prevailed 2-1 after extra time against Larne in the last four and last lifted the trophy in 2018

programme.title: Chronicles of the Sperrins
programme.desc: Walking guide Sean Harpur uncovers more of Gortin Glen Forest Parks hidden history as he and his guide dog Bennie go for their daily stroll, while teh restored clock is returned to Lissan Houses tower

programme.title: Paula McIntyres Hamely Kitchen
programme.desc: Paula makes the perfect comfort food - beef olives with onion gravy and creamy mash. She then drives to Kilkeel to meet two young broadcasters from Fuse FM, a community radio station. Against the stunning backdrop of the Mournes, Paula cooks monkfish with potato bread chips and a seaweed flavoured mayo. As a finale, she returns to her north-coast kitchen to make Ecclefechan tart and nutmeg custard

programme.title: Extraordinary Portraits
programme.desc: Featuring two sisters who share an extraordinary bond as one donated her womb to the other to enable her to give birth, making British medical history. Bill Bailey pairs Grace and Amy with Karen Turner, an award-winning artist with a powerful commitment to telling womens stories

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Dominic Littlewood and Melinda Messenger join Natasha Raskin Sharp and Tim Medhurst on a hunt and haggle for profitable antiques in Southampton. A cat terrifies one celebrity and a Viking find shocks the other

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Live coverage of the evening session on day 15 at the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield, featuring the fourth and final session of the second semi-final. Mark Williams claimed a hard-fought 17-14 victory over Judd Trump at this stage last year. He finished the match with a break of 123 and becoming the oldest player to reach the final of this event at the age of 50. With commentary and analysis from Steve Davis, Ken Doherty, Steven Hallworth, Stephen Hendry, John Parrott, Joe Perry, Dennis Taylor and Lee Walker

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: A selection of memorable performances that took place on the show in 1992. Including Kris Kross, Bizarre Inc, Manic Street Preachers and Primal Scream. Plus, the emergence of Take That, Mileys dad Billy Ray Cyru s getting the line-dancing going, and divas in the form of Annie Lennox and Mariah Carey

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: A selection of memorable Top of the Pops performances from the year. Dance music and Europop continued to dominate the charts with performances from Haddaway, Sub Sub, Ace of Base and Robin S, while the early stages of Britpop are represented by Suede and Radiohead. Icelandic superstar Bjork makes an early appearance and there are the big sellers by Meat Loaf and Gabrielle and hits by Jamiroquai and Chaka Demus and Pliers

programme.title: Mint
programme.desc: Cat has to make a speech during the racecourses Ladies Day event, and it forces her to confront what shes been denying about her relationship with Dylan. With alcohol and adrenaline in the air, its not long before rivalries are stirred up and best behaviour gives way to chaos. Shannon and Arran are caught in the crossfire as their families violently collide

programme.title: Mint
programme.desc: Dylan rages at Shannon in the wake of Ladies Day, but she escapes and spends a life-changing day with Arran. Meanwhile, tensions erupt between Cat and Dylan, making them question the foundations of their life together. Can they find a way to salvage what theyve built, and will the family survive what comes next?

programme.title: The Cage
programme.desc: Crime drama, starring Sheridan Smith and Michael Socha as two casino employees who catch each other trying to steal from their employer. They agree to both abandon their plans, but as their lives spiral out of control, the pact theyve made frays, setting them on a high-stakes crash course with gangsters, police, and each other

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Take a Hike
programme.desc: It is 40-year-old Cathys turn to lead the hike. She moved to the Isle of Mull three years ago, where she works as a technical manager in a biscuit factory. She has planned a coastal walk from the picture postcard town of Tobermory and has opted for some traditional Scottish activities to entertain the group

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.sub-title: Vale of Evesham: Bridging the Hungry Gap
programme.desc: Adam Henson and Sammi Kinghorn head to the Vale of Evesham, where fertile soils and a mild micro-climate have long made it one of Britains most productive growing regions, with a long history of producing food even in leaner months. Meanwhile, Charlotte Smith investigates how falling milk prices are leading some dairy farmers to call it a day, despite record exports and booming milk production

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Landward
programme.desc: Dougie Vipond visits a dairy farm near Torthorwald in Dumfries and Galloway to find out about the benefits of block calving, and Cammy Wilson is on home turf in Ayrshire as early potato planting gets under way. Shahbaz Majeed Shahbaz goes to see a seal colony in the Ythan estuary in Aberdeenshire and finds out how drones are being used for conversation, and Arlene Stuart is in Easter Ross to meet a woman raising awareness of the menopause

programme.title: Beechgrove Garden
programme.desc: Theres lots of planting for the plate at Beechgrove Garden just outside Aberdeen as Kirsty Wilson and Brian Cunningham start growing a wide variety of edible produce. Kirsty starts work on her competition plot, while Brian gives an update on the vegetables he sowed at the start of the series and is in his rain garden checking on what survived the wet weather in north-east Scotland this year. Plus, the usual handy hints and a visit to Calum Clunies allotment in Leven

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Best Bites
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt presents more highlights, with recipes from Poppy OToole, Joe Hurd, Ols Halas and Jack Croft and Will Murray, while comedian Greg Davies faces his food heaven or hell

programme.title: Andi Olivers Fabulous Feasts
programme.desc: Andi helps Jasper Thompson throws a party with a Caribbean flavour, to launch a community cafe at a pioneering Bristol homeless project that turns shipping containers into temporary housing. The chef must put together a feast that draws on her and Jaspers own roots in Antigua and Jamaica, whilst paying tribute to Bristols wider Caribbean heritage, using local produce

programme.title: Grand Hotel (1932)
programme.desc: During an eventful few days, the luxurious Grand Hotel in Berlin hosts the intersecting lives of a diverse group of guests, including a Russian ballerina fearful that her career is waning, a penniless baron, a terminally ill accountant and a powerful Prussian businessman desperate to make a deal. Melodrama, starring Greta Garbo, John Barrymore, Joan Crawford and Wallace Beery

programme.title: Weather Watchers with Barra Best
programme.desc: Part two of two. The weather presenter and journalist examines how climate shapes everyones lives and looks at the activities that it enables people to participate in

programme.title: Flog It!

programme.desc: An Ulster Senior Football Championship semi-final (Throw-in 4.00pm). Sarah Mulkerrins presents coverage from a venue to be confirmed, as Fermanagh or Armagh face Donegal or Down. programme.title: The Chronicles of Armagh

programme.title: Paula McIntyres Hamely Kitchen

She meets a Highland dance teacher and her father in Bready, near Strabane, both novice cooks, and shows them how to make the perfect barbecue lamb kebabs with a minty broadbean dip. There is a touch of nostalgia about the final pudding of the series, as Paula prepares individual brown lemonade cakes filled with a gooseberry and elderflower cream

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Hazel Irvine presents live coverage of the second session of the final on day 16 at the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield. After struggling in the opening session, Mark Williams produced an improved display in the evening 12 months ago, but still trailed Zhao Xintong 11-6, and no player had ever recovered from an overnight deficit of five or more frames in a world final at this venue. With commentary and analysis from Steve Davis, Ken Doherty, Steven Hallworth, Stephen Hendry, John Parrott, Joe Perry, Dennis Taylor and Lee Walker

programme.title: Hell for Leather: The Story of Gaelic Football
programme.desc: Armagh legend Oisín McConville reveals the magic and mayhem that led Tyrone, Donegal, and Armagh to All-Ireland victory, while the Cork ladies become one of the games greatest teams ever. Plus, the crucial influence of managers and mentors, planners and motivators who make champions of their teams and reinvent the game year on year

programme.title: Sunday Politics Northern Ireland
programme.desc: Mark Carruthers looks at the political developments of the week and questions policy-makers on the key issues

On April 6, 1917, Lance Corporal Tom Blake and Lance Corporal William Schofield receive orders from General Erinmore to prevent Colonel Mackenzie from leading The 2nd Devons into a trap set by the Germans. The soldiers must proceed on foot into enemy territory to deliver the message, which will stop 1,600 men from walking straight into a deadly trap. Sam Mendes war drama, starring Dean-Charles Chapman, George MacKay, Daniel Mays and Colin Firth

programme.title: Here We Are (2020)
programme.desc: When his autistic son is due to move into a home, a devoted father decides to hit the road and run away with his son, delaying their separation and pushing the two on a journey of self discovery. Drama, with Shai Avivi, Noam Imber and Smadi Wolfman. In Hebrew

The story then goes back in time to reveal how two men, who formed such a close bond years before, came to such a point. Drama, written by and starring Richard Gadd, with Jamie Bell

programme.desc: A woman calls 999 to report that her 11-year-old daughter returned from staying at her dads house and claimed that a stranger came into her bedroom in the night and sexually assaulted her. programme.desc: Series following colourful life in a large pawn shop. From alligators to false limbs, its staff has no idea what could come through the door next, and profits come with a huge risk. Thursday

programme.desc: Only a few days remain in the season, and the captains battle it out in one of the tightest climaxes featured in the show

programme.title: Wicked Tuna

programme.title: Swamp People: Serpent Invasion
programme.desc: Troy and Pickle receive a tip about a snake pit. Meanwhile, Bill and Tes investigate a quarry full of pythons and Dusty has a close call with the supernatural

programme.desc: Three hardcore mining teams battle it out to unearth the most gold. At Scratchy Moo, Matt and Damo weigh up the two offers they have received for their land

programme.title: Gold Wars Downunder
programme.desc: The sale of gold is an important source of income for hundreds of thousands of Australians, including the men and women willing to risk it all. In this series, three hardcore mining teams battle it out on the one patch of gold-rich-land to see who can unearth the biggest nuggets and the most gold in an epic made-for-television gold-hunting season

programme.title: Ancient Aliens
programme.desc: How, across the globe, reports of mysterious glowing orbs are on the rise. They move in impossible ways and show up near sensitive military sites. Their origin and purpose remain unknown, raising questions about potential extraterrestrial contact

programme.title: The Proof Is Out There
programme.sub-title: Worm in the Sky, Skeletal Mystery Remains and Thessaloniki Mystery Sounds
programme.desc: A remotely operated vehicle (ROV) finds the remains of a massive skeleton 825 metres below the surface of the Mediterranean. Plus, a building that appears to shrink as a family gets closer to it and a strange worm-shaped object spotted in the sky

programme.title: The Proof Is Out There
programme.sub-title: Spontaneous Human Combustion, the Tell-Tale Hum, and UFO Explosion
programme.desc: Tony Harris and his team of experts see the black and white photographs taken in 1951 that depict a woman in St Petersburg, Florida, spontaneously combusting. Plus, a brain-numbing noise plaguing residents in Brooklyn and a woman who claims to have witnessed something even more frightening than a Chinese spy balloon

programme.title: The Proof Is Out There
programme.sub-title: Teen UFO, Flying Dinosaur Over Idaho, and Headless Goats
programme.desc: A photograph taken by a Swedish teenager said to offer some of the most convincing UFO evidence ever. Plus, a video posted online showing a pterodactyl-like creature flying over Idaho and the reason headless goats are repeatedly being dumped in an Atlanta River

programme.title: What on Earth?
programme.desc: The series examines six more mysterious satellite images, including a possible smokescreen over North Korea, and two forgotten pyramids in Egypt

programme.title: What on Earth?
programme.desc: Nasa astronaut Mike Barratt describes taking a photograph of a strange ring in the ice of the worlds oldest lake, while working on the International Space Station

programme.title: What on Earth?
programme.desc: Photographs illustrate how the barely inhabited Australian Outback seems to light up like a network of vast cities when viewed from space

programme.title: Secret Societies: In the Shadows
programme.desc: While the Illuminati secret order was disbanded in 1785, many believe this elite group still hold the power and pull strings from behind closed doors

programme.title: Secret Societies: In the Shadows
programme.desc: Skull and Bones, also known as The Order, Order 322 or The Brotherhood of Death, is an undergraduate senior secret student society at Yale University in New Haven, Connecticut. It is a pinnacle of the elite, and its members have become presidents, Supreme Court justices and heads of industry. To be tapped is to be welcomed into a line of power brokers with the promise of life-long opportunities

programme.title: Secret Societies: In the Shadows
programme.desc: A look at Le Cercle, which started in the post-war years as a Catholic conservative group built to broker European rapprochement. It now operates as a shadow intelligence agency rumoured to control world politics

programme.title: Holy Marvels with Dennis Quaid
programme.desc: From the burials of biblical heroes to the elaborate practices of ancient Egypt, Dennis Quaid delves into the fascinating customs that have marked humanitys passage from life to death. He examines the rituals surrounding death and the afterlife and how different cultures have honoured their dead throughout history. Highlighting the significance of these rituals, he sheds light on how they reflect the beliefs and values of various societies

programme.title: Battle Treasures with Foxy and Bruce
programme.desc: Jason Fox and Bruce Crompton examine the rifles that changed modern warfare, including the German STG 44, historys first assault rifle, which revolutionised infantry tactics

programme.title: Battle Treasures with Foxy and Bruce
programme.desc: Blades once decided the fate of nations. Jason Fox and Bruce Crompton handle a Viking sword, an artefact forged for raiders who terrorised the coasts of Europe. They then reveal Tantia Tophees sword, wielded by an Indian legend

programme.title: Strange Evidence
programme.desc: A security camera captures a ghostly figure stalking a Japanese taxi stand. Locals fear that even viewing the footage will put their lives in danger

programme.title: Paranormal: Caught on Camera
programme.desc: Documentary the most chilling and unexplainable paranormal activity footage captured by eyewitnesses

programme.title: National Park Mysteries
programme.desc: A trip to Great Smoky Mountains National Park, which straddles the border between North Carolina and Tennessee. It is the alleged underground base for a powerful secret society

programme.title: National Park Mysteries
programme.desc: A trip to Mark Twain National Forest, located in the southern half of Missouri. Some claim that the dark, mysterious energies that surround this national park point to treasures from a lost civilisation that is hidden there

programme.title: Alien Files: Unsealed
programme.desc: With science closer than ever to proving that life once lived on Mars, this programme examines whether there is a chance that something once exisited - or may still remain - there

programme.title: Alien Files: Unsealed
programme.desc: The vast distances in space, and the prolonged amount of time it takes for communications to be transmitted and received between distant stars

programme.desc: The retailers scrutinise a signature apparently made by deposed Iraqi leader Saddam Hussein, and Les loses his cool with a customer over a broken necklace. Documentary series following the daily running of a pawn shop

programme.desc: A customer brings in an Olympic torch used in the relay for the 2002 Winter Games, an event held in Salt Lake City, Utah

programme.title: Hardcore Pawn

programme.title: Hardcore Pawn
programme.desc: A man tries to sell a series of poorly stuffed animals, leading to to an unusual threat being made against Les

programme.title: Hardcore Pawn
programme.desc: A woman sells an artistic statue of an old woman in a bathing suit, but when Les buys it, Seth blames Ashley for contributing to the purchase

programme.title: Hardcore Pawn

programme.title: Junk and Disorderly
programme.desc: Henry and Skid are granted access to the back of the Cotswolds Motor Museum

programme.title: Junk and Disorderly
programme.desc: Enthusiasts scour the country, challenging themselves to make money out of buying and selling other peoples automotive junk and classic collectibles

programme.title: Pawn Stars
programme.desc: Rick checks out a rare playing card featuring a legendary surfer. Chum heads to the range to check out a Yellow Boy carbine. Corey looks through a collection of items from Van Halens Hot for Teacher music video

programme.title: Pawn Stars
programme.desc: Rick seeks a good deal for a painting supposedly created by artist Rene Magritte. In order to have it authenticated, he and Chum must head to Belgium. Corey tries to score on a Tom Brady Pro Bowl jersey and Seiko watch

programme.title: Container Wars
programme.desc: At the port of Los Angeles, the Uzi and Shlomi are out to destroy the competition. Two Israeli auctioneers come up with a plan to beat their rivals in Los Angeles

programme.title: Container Wars
programme.desc: Matt makes fun of his rivals when their expensive purchases turn out to be junk, but may get a taste of his own medicine when he bets on a container he thinks will make him rich

programme.title: Container Wars
programme.desc: Uzi and Shlomi make a risky gamble in the series biggest auction to date

programme.title: Container Wars

programme.title: Container Wars

programme.desc: Manic Matt competes with Uzi and Schlomi over a container from Guam

programme.title: Storage Wars

programme.title: Storage Wars
programme.sub-title: Two for the Price of Dust
programme.desc: The big guns come out in Riverside, but Rene isnt having any of it. Darrell presses his luck with a smaller locker and makes a neat profit. Dusty finds that once he gets going, its hard to stop - so he just keeps re-loading

programme.title: Storage Wars
programme.sub-title: Its a Safe Time to Get Lit!
programme.desc: Lisa heads to Huntington Beach, Darrell spends the right amount of time going through his locker, Kenny rides a recent string of luck right into his unit, while Rene puts his bankroll up against a pair of safes

programme.title: Storage Wars
programme.sub-title: All Hail, King Brandi: The Rule-Her of West Covina
programme.desc: In West Covina, Brandi makes a serious land grab and it pays off handsomely. Rene cant resist the mystery of a good safe. Kenny cant seem to satisfy his hunger, while Barry takes an opportunity to poach from the competition

programme.title: Storage Wars
programme.desc: Ivy scores a packed locker and stumbles across an antique of unusual origin. Darrell grabs a smaller unit, but finds that good things sometimes come in petite packages

programme.title: Beyond Skinwalker Ranch
programme.desc: In an effort to gain a broader perspective and build larger data sets, the Skinwalker team adds two team members and takes the offensive by pursuing evidence of similar phenomena found at analogous sites around the country

programme.title: Curse of Skinwalker Ranch
programme.desc: A stunning UAP - Unidentified Anomalous Phenomena - appears above Skinwalker Walker Ranch during an experiment involving hundreds of illuminated drones. The timing of what happens next leaves the team members in disbelief

programme.title: Hazardous History with Henry Winkler
programme.desc: A nostalgic journey through Americas past reveals shocking everyday activities and products that were once normal but are now unthinkable in modern society. In this episode, he considers toys and daredevil playground antics that once defined growing up, uncovering explosive science kits, high-flying kite tubes, dangerous waterslides - and more

programme.title: The Witches of Salem
programme.desc: How paranoia regarding the suprnatural reached fever pitch as the demonic conspiracy terrorising Salem village grew, leaving no one safe. Boston jail filled with suspected witches and those accused faced execution if found guilty. Narrated by Mark Strong

programme.title: Historys Greatest Mysteries
programme.desc: After centuries of searching, Blackbeards epic hoard remains missing. As new evidence emerges, the mystery endures as to what became of the treasure

programme.title: Legends of the Pharaohs
programme.desc: Wife to King Akhenaton, Nefertitis portrait bust - now located in Berlin - is one of the most well-known ancient sculptures in existence. Nefertiti means the beautiful woman has come, and according to 14th-century BC rumours, she lived up to her name

programme.title: The Proof Is Out There
programme.sub-title: Worm in the Sky, Skeletal Mystery Remains and Thessaloniki Mystery Sounds
programme.desc: A remotely operated vehicle (ROV) finds the remains of a massive skeleton 825 metres below the surface of the Mediterranean. Plus, a building that appears to shrink as a family gets closer to it and a strange worm-shaped object spotted in the sky

programme.title: The Proof Is Out There
programme.sub-title: Spontaneous Human Combustion, the Tell-Tale Hum, and UFO Explosion
programme.desc: Tony Harris and his team of experts see the black and white photographs taken in 1951 that depict a woman in St Petersburg, Florida, spontaneously combusting. Plus, a brain-numbing noise plaguing residents in Brooklyn and a woman who claims to have witnessed something even more frightening than a Chinese spy balloon

programme.title: The Proof Is Out There
programme.sub-title: Swamp Beast Sighting, Martian Conspiracy and Transparent Mexican Sea Monster
programme.desc: An investigation into a remarkable sighting of the skunk ape, a creature often likened to Bigfoot. A canoer claims to have captured footage of this elusive beast navigating through gator-infested waters, raising questions about what may be lurking in the swamps. Plus, the discovery of a peculiar transparent sea monster off the coast of Mexico, which has sparked the curiosity of marine enthusiasts and experts alike

programme.title: Britains Most Historic Towns
programme.desc: Alice Roberts tells the story of Tudor England by studying the history of Norwich, learning the harsh reality of religious intolerance, uncovering the origins of Norwich FCs iconic canary and experiencing humiliating Tudor justice. Aerial archaeologist Ben Robinson takes to the skies to see how the current layout reflects its history and, at the conclusion of the episode, cutting edge CGI is used to recreate how Norwich would have looked during this period of British history

Armando Iannucci and Joel Dommett both find unusual substitutes for sweets and the normally infallible Alex Horne makes a rare, condiment-based error for which he will no doubt be punished by Taskmaster Greg Davies

programme.title: Formula 1
programme.sub-title: 2026 Miami Grand Prix Sprint Qualifying Highlights
programme.desc: Alex Jacques presents action from Miami International Autodrome in Florida, where the drivers battled for grid positions ahead of the sprint race at the fourth round of the season

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: The fiery chef tries to change the fortunes of Finn McCools, a traditional Irish pub in the Hamptons, New York. The chances of the establishment becoming a success are being ruined by the owner Buddy Mazzios two sons, bartender Jason and chef Brian, who are constantly fighting with each other, prompting Gordon to have a heart-to-heart with the family

programme.title: Travel Man: 48 Hours In
programme.desc: Joe Lycett heads to the Austro-German border with his guest vacationer Roisin Conaty, spending a couple of days in the fairy-tale city of Salzburg. After checking in to the art-filled Blaue Ganz hotel, they hop into a horse and carriage for a tour of the city and an on-the-go three-course meal. They then embark on a forest-bathing workshop, attempting a series of exercises to reawaken their senses. Day two starts with a homage to Mozart. The duo reflect on the composers music then sample the citys signature sweet, Mozartkulgen - aka Mozart Balls - a mix of nougat, marzipan and dark chocolate

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In north London, Helen and her Albanian boyfriend David kick off the contest with their Greek menu. Their food goes down well with Sara and her Aussie husband Thierry, which is more than can be said for Davids views on womens roles within the home. On day two, Vicky and Geoff cook up an Italian menu, but their rock and pop idol dress code is a huge flop with one couple. The hosts face a grilling over food shortcuts, and Helen and Vicky go head to head in the battle of the cheesecakes. Finally, Sara and Thierry serve up the fashions and food of the 1980s. However, one guest makes a fashion faux pas about Saras favourite frock

programme.title: Kirsties House of Craft

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition kicks off at Whod a Thought It in Grafty Green, Kent, where partners Joe Mallett and Alex Rea Baldwin hope to impress their fellow guests. On arrival, Liz and Brian are excited over their two hot tubs, and Jackie and Paul are more than happy with the complimentary champagne. However, Matthew and Liam are underwhelmed by the cleanliness levels

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The owners visit the New Osborne Hotel in Blackpool on their second night, where proprietor Matthew Bracegirdle and entertainment manager Liam hope their guests feel at home in their family friendly establishment. However, Jackie and Paul are unimpressed by their small room, and Joe and Alex find the cleanliness levels lacking

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The third visit of the week is to the Fairy Falls Hotel in Trefriw, Conwy County Borough, where BandB newcomers Jackie and Paul Langshaw hope to impress the other contestants. They take their guests to a local maze in the afternoon and, after an uncomfortable nights sleep for Alex, some of the breakfast orders are misplaced

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Husband and wife Liz and Brian Jardine welcome their guests to The Great Trewern BandB, Llandrindod Wells, Powys, where they enjoy the quiet life. Joe and Alex feel far away from it all, but the shared bathroom facilities fail to impress the other guests. At dinner, talk turns to first impressions and it appears it is all water under the bridge for Jackie and Joe, but she remains upset with the feedback Matthew and Liam gave her

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The BandB owners meet one final time to find out what they have been paid. Joe Mallett and Alex Rea Baldwin kick things off by confronting Matthew Bracegirdle and Liam Halewood about their comments regarding the cleanliness of their establishment, and Jackie and Paul Langshaw complain about their poor breakfast score

programme.title: The King of Queens
programme.desc: Doug asks his father for financial aid to help deal with the mould problem in his house. When he receives some paternal advice on saving for a rainy day, he starts to wonder where all his money goes - and decides Carries wild spending is the problem. Comedy, starring Kevin James

programme.title: The King of Queens

programme.desc: Daphne helps Frasier make up his mind whether he should sneak an on-air endorsement for a Chinese restaurant into his radio show. Comedy, starring Kelsey Grammer and Jane Leeves

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Homer finds himself being rushed to hospital suffering from food poisoning as a consequence of Apus misconduct at the Kwik-E-Mart. Featuring the guest voice of James Woods

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Mr Burns sets out to be reunited with his beloved teddy bear, which he lost as a child. Unfortunately, it is now owned by Maggie, presenting Homer with an unenviable dilemma. Featuring the voices of the Ramones

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Bart lands a job as assistant to his idol Krusty the Clown and even gets to say his own catchphrase in front of the cameras, winning instant celebrity status. Featuring the guest voice of TV host Conan OBrien

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Professional singer Barry and hairdresser Angela, who live in Gloucestershire, are planning to relocate to the area around Benidorm. Jasmine Harman lines up five options for their budget of £225,000, hoping that at least one will tempt them into making an offer

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The first venue of the week is Sawcliffe Manor in High Risby, Lincolnshire. An afternoon at the onsite spa leaves the guests relaxed, but a lack of vegan breakfast stirs up trouble

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second visit of the week is to Charlotte and Donnas Rooms at the Inn, in Retford. Things get off to a rough start when Rob discovers some dusty furniture in his room, but a visit to a recreation of a Native American camp shakes things up. At feedback time, the hosts are left with earache over complaints about night-time noises

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The BandBers visit Robs Wolds Wine Estate n Nottinghamshire, which features glamping pods in the middle of a vineyard. Everyone is happy at the wine tasting but a surprise chicken invasion at breakfast leaves Robs guests less than impressed and his confidence is knocked when he receives some unpleasant cleanliness comments in his feedback

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The guest house owners visit Dovestone Holiday Park in Saddleworth, Greater Manchester, where host Karlie hopes her lavish lodges and glorious views will be a winning combination. Things get off to a sizzling start with the BandBers heralding the hot tubs, but not everyone is onboard with a group meditation session in the afternoon. The local produce at breakfast goes down a storm - but one pair moaning about their mattress makes for a less-than comfortable experience for Karlie come feedback time

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants meet one last time to discover how much they have been paid by their fellow hoteliers and to air their grievances. There are bust-ups about breakfast options, ding-dongs over decor and grumblings about gravy-scented wine. After the dust has settled, all participants learn the identity of the winner

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Catching up with two sets of house-hunters who battled the property markets either side of the Thames. In 2018, Emily and Dustin had saved a healthy deposit, but in the north London market, they were finding it hard to secure their first home. In 2009, it was all about location for Sarah and Ian. Fixated on their beloved Balham, but without the budget for two bedrooms, Phil Spencer had to employ some creative thinking in order to fulfil their wish list. Now he returns to find out how they have got on since cameras stopped rolling

programme.title: Kirstie and Phils Love It or List It
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp heads back to Albrighton in Shropshire to catch up with Jamie Lea and Curtis, a couple whose Love It or List It journey began in 2019 with their three-bed terrace house that was a full-blown battleground. Jamie Lea dreamed of putting down roots, focusing on family life with daughter Scarlett and preparing for baby number two but Curtis had other ideas. Ever restless, he was glued to property sites, hunting for their next fixer-upper: a bigger home with a sprawling countryside garden for their growing family. Kirstie swooped in with a daring £30,000 Love It plan, and the couple rolled up their sleeves, stretching every penny with a mix of DIY grit and determination. Meanwhile, armed with a £280,000 List It budget, Phil uncovered some jaw-dropping homes - including a dreamy four-bed project - that left them seriously torn. Then, in 2023, Phil revisited and found the couple stuck in selling limbo. Never one to shy away from tough love, he dished out pearls of wisdom to help get them back on track. Now, Kirstie uncovers the next chapter in Jamie Lea and Curtiss roller coaster story. Did they finally manage to break free from their stalemate, sell their fully renovated terraced home and achieve their happily-ever-after?

programme.title: Channel 4 News
programme.desc: Including sport and weather

programme.title: Secret Africa: Into the Wild
programme.desc: The danger cranks up as Lucy Shepherd and her nomadic hunter-gatherer team head through the Serengeti to a sacred volcano. As they cross paths with some of the wilds most dangerous predators, with prides of lions stalking nearby, their water supplies run dangerously low. The team must stay vigilant, relying on their skills to sense danger before it strikes

programme.title: Worlds Most Secret Hotels
programme.desc: An old Cornwall jail becomes an extraordinary luxury retreat, and opulent ancient caves bring the South African wilderness up close. A historic Swedish lighthouse on the rugged rocks of Paternoster Island shines a light for luxury seekers, while in tropical northern Queensland, an eco-resort offers stunning treetop accommodation

programme.title: Beast (2022)
programme.desc: Recently widowed Nate Samuels and his teenage daughters Meredith and Norah find their holiday at a South African nature reserve turns into a nightmare when they are hunted by a huge rogue lion in the savanna. Action thriller, starring Idris Elba, Sharlto Copley, Leah Jeffries and Iyana Halley

programme.title: Assassin Club (2023)
programme.desc: Assassin-for-hire Morgan prepares to turn his back on contract killings to pursue romance with schoolteacher Sophie, who is unaware of his true calling with a snipers rifle. The hitman accepts a lucrative farewell pay day brokered by his mentor, Caldwell - six separate contracts, each worth $1million. However, Morgans targets are also assassins hired to kill him. Thriller, starring Henry Golding, Daniela Melchior, Sam Neill and Noomi Rapace

programme.title: Free Guy (2021)
programme.desc: Guy lives a fairly monotonous life as a bank employee. That is until he meets the girl of his dreams and realises hes a non-player character within a massive open world video game. Does he deviate from the life hes always lived or does he follow his dream girl into the unknown future? Adventure comedy, starring Ryan Reynolds, Jodie Comer, Taika Waititi and Lil Rel Howery

programme.title: Formula 1
programme.sub-title: 2026 Miami Grand Prix Sprint and Qualifying Highlights
programme.desc: Lee McKenzie and Alice Powell present action from the second sprint race of the season and the battle for grid positions in the fourth round of the campaign at Miami International Autodrome

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: The fiery chef heads to the Seascape restaurant in the village of Islip on Long Island, New York, in a bid to change the fortunes of its owner Peter Matthews, who is nearly $1million in debt. Gordon is dismayed to discover unprotected meat and fish left next to mouldy pesto jars in the kitchen and plans to close down the eatery until radical changes are made

programme.title: Travel Man: 48 Hours In
programme.desc: Joe Lycett is joined by The Last Leg presenter Alex Brooker for a two-day tour of the Dutch city of Rotterdam. After taking the train direct from London, they launch into a break full of unusual activities, attractions and flavours. For accommodation, they swerve hotels in favour of Wikkelboats; eco-friendly floating houses insulated with corrugated cardboard, that come complete with hot tubs on deck. Once theyre settled in, they book onto the Rooftop tour, taking in some of the most interesting roofs and views in Rotterdam, including a farm and the oldest skyscraper in Europe. For dinner, they head to Fermin - a restaurant that loves to use fermented flavours in its unique and inventive menu. Day two begins with a culture hit as they head to Rotterdams largest gallerys storeroom, the futuristic Depot, before rounding off their caper with chips in peanut sauce, a stroll across a famous bridge and a game at the Dutch pinball museum, which gives Alex an idea for a whole new show

programme.title: Sarah Beenys New Country Lives
programme.desc: The property expert follows plucky country life newbies through the highs, lows and laughs, as they embrace their fresh starts in beautiful rural retreats. In this opening episode she meets James, who is creating a boutique country hotel, while Gary and Annette enter their village scarecrow competition

programme.title: Sunday Brunch
programme.desc: Highlights of the food and chat show, with Tim Lovejoy and Simon Rimmer

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Bart lands a job as assistant to his idol Krusty the Clown and even gets to say his own catchphrase in front of the cameras, winning instant celebrity status. Featuring the guest voice of TV host Conan OBrien
