programme.title: Emergency Helicopter Medics
programme.desc: Air ambulance crew members take drastic steps to reset a patients dangerously fast heartbeat, a cyclist crashes in a remote area, and a motorist is injured in a road accident

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: A 43-year-old cyclist is brought to AandE after crashing face first into a car, with injuries are so severe that he was put into an induced coma at the scene. Meanwhile, 68-year-old Pat awaits the results of an X-ray after injuring her leg on holiday, and 89-year-old Adrian complains of chest pain

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: Beth, 68, is rushed to St Georges after falling down a flight of stairs and badly breaking her ankle, meaning she faces emergency surgery and intensive physiotherapy to walk again. Leigh, 67, is admitted with head injuries after being knocked off his bicycle in a hit-and-run incident and medics are concerned he has suffered a severe trauma to his head as he was not wearing a helmet, while 26-year-old Luke arrives in AandE after an accident at work involving an electric saw

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Jimmy Doherty visits Europes biggest brewery - Heineken in the Netherlands - on a mission to explain the difference between lager and ale. His journey takes a surprising twist when he comes face to face with the mother of all lagers. Kate Quilton sets Matt Tebbutt the challenge of making crumpets, and finds out how they get filled with unique holes at the Warburtons factory in Enfield, north London. Matt also heads for snowy Canada to discover why maple syrup is so expensive, and finds out that maple syrup robberies are a big concern to the industry

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Themis and Paolo have been visiting the unspoilt beaches of Kapparis in Cyprus for the past six years and now they want to buy their own holiday home. Jasmine Harman lines up five properties for their £180,000 budget

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The first visit of the week is Nateby Inn in Cumbria, where landlady Gaynor Walker is ready with a warm welcome. A trip to Gaynors local brewery turns out to be a barrel of laughs when one guest beats another in a cask-rolling competition, before a broken cooker creates chaos in the kitchen the following morning

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The owners visit the Outfields Farm Bed and Breakfast in Ravensden, Bedfordshire, on their second stay, where proprietors Graham and Julia Ashley treat them to an afternoon of indoor sky-diving before hosting a dinner filled with surprises. The guests enjoy a good nights sleep, but one of the owners is expecting hassle at breakfast time

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The third visit of the week is to At Home in Chiswick, west London, where artist Bob Osbourne appears to take a rather laid-back approach to running his BandB. On arrival, guests are pleasantly surprised by their first impressions of the communal living space-come-mini art gallery - but its not long before they are dishing the dirt

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition draws to a close at the Ammonite Lodge guesthouse in Chard, Somerset, where owners Andrew and Clare Ashmore hope to excite their guests with a game of skittles. During the final dinner of the week, the competitors discuss their first impressions of each other, and Bob and Julia share some amusing observations with the group

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The BandB owners confront their critics face to face, and the winners are announced. Nateby Inn host Gaynor takes issue with Graham and Julias reduced marks for cobwebs. The conversation becomes heated when Outfields Farms owners tackle Gaynors complaints in return, and Georgie jumps to their defence. Artist Bob Osborne has a long list of grievances he wants to address, and Julia is left cringing when her previous comments rebound on her

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Jonathan Broom and his wife Deborah have put everything on hold while he pursues his dream of building a miniature Hollywood Hills-style mansion. They stumble across a scrap of land in north London, but its fraught with problems and the only way they can complete the £1million home is by sinking half of it six metres below ground. Surrounded on all sides by their neighbours back gardens, the couple have to dismantle walls and fences and promise to put them back together again, and thats just the start of their difficulties. Kevin McCloud follows their progress

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud meets a master craftsman whose dream is to build a castle in rural Devon made entirely of mud. Kevin McCabe plans to form the huge property out of two vast curved roundhouses - the largest of which is inspired by the natural geometry of a snail shell - connected by glazing and topped with wildflower meadow roofs to mirror the surrounding countryside. However, the project involves mixing and laying a mind-boggling 2,000 tons of cob, all during warm, sunny weather - and the sheer scale of the task soon becomes clear

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Carol longs to buy a holiday home for her and her husband on the island of Lanzarote that will work for all four generations of their family. She particularly loves the east coast, with its buzzing resorts of Costa Teguise and Puerto del Carmen, and is looking for a town house or apartment with a pool and a view from the terrace. With a budget of £150,000 - and Carols daughter-in-law-Donna coming along to help - Jasmine Harman sets out to find a property to please everyone

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: PR manager Emmy Webster kicks off a week of dinner parties in Yeovil, Somerset, where she hopes her locally sourced produce will impress her guests - self-proclaimed country bumpkin Mel Dunford, used-car salesman Andy Neil, radio broadcaster Steve Carpenter and businessman Alan Bunter - and make her an early contender for the £1,000 prize. However, she is caught out when her hollandaise sauce is found to have been bought from a shop

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: DJ Steve Carpenter hosts the second dinner party in Yeovil, Somerset, hoping his country-style cooking and carefully sourced ingredients will win him the £1,000 cash prize. However, the entertainer lets his focus slip when he allows an unwanted ingredient onto one plate

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Hosting the third dinner party in Yeovil is diminutive fork-lift truck driver Mel Dunford. With an exotic menu - including duck vindaloo, which is her own invention - Mel hopes to prove her doubters wrong. And its not just her surprise culinary skills that wow her guests - she also breaks into song, leaving everyone astounded

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Businessman Alan Bunter hosts the fourth dinner party in the Yeovil area of Somerset, hoping that his enthusiasm for all things Japanese will help propel him into the lead. However, car salesman Andy Neil and DJ Steve Carpenter fail to be influenced by the calm atmosphere and spend the evening arguing. As the ladies at the table struggle to get a word in, the menu of rotten beans, tofu cheesecake and soggy noodles fails to impress

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Last to host in Yeovil is 50-year-old used-car dealer Andy Neil, who is supremely confident that his fine dining experience, along with the beautiful surroundings of his home, will bag him the £1,000 prize. Six amuse-bouches for his starter are a visual and culinary treat, but his constant questions and need for feedback and praise threaten to put off his fellow diners. He also has to contend with local radio personality Steve Carpenter jockeying for position as top dog of the group, and fussy eater Mel Dunford, who is not afraid to tell him exactly what she thinks of his fancy food

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: This weeks competition begins at Newquay Beach Hotel, where the guests spend an afternoon surfing and a revelation leaves one set of hosts unsure about their chances

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Mum and daughter Mandy and Elly host the weeks second visit at Devon Heaven Hideaways, where they offer a touch of bliss at their plush glampsite. First impressions are excellent, everyone enjoys a trip to a castle, and a riverside breakfast is a roaring success. So why do the hosts end up shocked by their scores?

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: For visit three of the week, the BandBers head to Ceredigion, where married couple Stuart and Amy offer a great big slice of vintage Americana at their Tin Can Retreat. Stuart and Amy get everyone revved up with an afternoon of electric biking, but are left eating dust after some cleanliness complaints in their feedback

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Steve and Mandy host the weeks final visit at The Thorverton Arms in Devon. Their classic country pub seems to put a smile on everyones faces - until things get hairy for one pair. The hosts hope a belly-busting breakfast can swing things back in their favour

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: BandB owners take it in turns as hosts to decide which of their establishments provides the best value for money. The weeks scores are revealed and the winner announced

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: Fifty-year-old motorcyclist Michelle is rushed to AandE after crashing head first into a lamp-post, with scans revealing that she has broken bones in her neck, arm and shoulder and requires emergency surgery. Timothy, 69, arrives at St Georges after riding his bicycle into the back of a parked ambulance, while 33-year-old expectant mother Eleanor is admitted after cutting her thumb on a meat cleaver

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: Seventy-one-year-old Tony is rushed to AandE by air ambulance after falling head first onto a tiled floor while doing DIY in his kitchen. A 10-year-old girl, Lucy, arrives at St Georges after having seizures for the past 48 hours

programme.desc: The air ambulance teams battle to save cyclist Grahams partially amputated leg, while Rose is freed from a rolled car in Buckinghamshire. Gary suffers a rare and extremely serious complete knee dislocation playing football, but strong pain relief results in some unexpected hilarity

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Charity fundraiser Rachel Speight-McGregor kicks off a week of festive dinner parties in York. programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Junior doctor Gyles Morrison serves a uniquely Caribbean take on a traditional Christmas dinner. programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In the third edition from York, vegetarian host Nicola Gregory hopes to convert four carnivores with her completely meat-free festive menu. Meanwhile, charity fundraiser Rachel Speight-McGregor is more interested in drinking shots of salad dressing, and barista Harry Baines is concerned about his gift of a furry festive jock strap

programme.title: Come Dine with Me

programme.title: Come Dine with Me

programme.title: 1066: A Year to Conquer England
programme.desc: Docudrama in which Dan Snow explores the political intrigues and family betrayals between Vikings, Anglo-Saxons and Normans that led to the Battle of Hastings. The first edition reveals how the death of Edward the Confessor without an heir triggered a bitter race to succeed him as king of England, with Earl Harold successfully taking the crown. However, William of Normandy believes the throne has been promised to him and decides to invade, while Viking king Harald Hardrada also fancies himself as monarch

programme.title: 1066: A Year to Conquer England
programme.desc: As Dan Snows docudrama continues, King Harold has to take on two invasion forces. First, his brother Tostig attacks the south coast. He is repelled, but there is more to come. Later in the year, a vast Viking invasion force led by King Harald Hardrada of Norway lands in the north of England. Harold rushes to Stamford Bridge to fight for his kingdom and for his life

programme.title: 1066: A Year to Conquer England
programme.desc: After months trapped in a port in northern France, the winds finally change and Duke William leads his vast invasion fleet across the Channel. Catastrophically, King Harold is 300 miles away in the north, having defeated the Vikings. Harold rushes back south, gathers a new army, and heads to Hastings to take on this new invasion. The two armies will meet at Battle in Sussex on October 14, 1066, and the outcome will change the shape of England and Europe for ever. Dan Snow presents

programme.title: Witness to a Massacre: Nanjing 1937
programme.desc: The story of the Japanese invasion of Nanjing through evidence smuggled out of the city by those who refused to flee in a heroic effort to help civilians

programme.title: Digging Up Britains Past
programme.desc: In 1592, Elizabeth I visited Sudeley Castle in the Cotswolds for a three-day party commemorating the nations victory over the Spanish Armada. Here, Raksha Dave, Alex Langlands and assorted volunteers and archaeologists hunt for a lost garden planted as part of the celebrations, while there is also a visit to Kenilworth Castle to investigate an Elizabethan garden still in full bloom today. Plus, a look at how the gardens Tudor owner, Robert Dudley transformed his estate to win favour with his Queen

programme.title: Digging Up Britains Past
programme.desc: Archaeologists and presenters Raksha Dave and Alex Langlands join a team from Reading University at a site in Silchester, near Reading, as they examine one of the best-preserved Roman sites in Britain

programme.title: Digging Up Britains Past
programme.sub-title: Invincible - The Royal Navys Greatest Ship
programme.desc: Raksha Dave and Alex Langlands examine the history of HMS Invincible, which was captured from the French in 1747 and helped transform the Royal Navy with the lessons it taught about organisation and ship design. For more than 260 years, HMS Invincible lay at the bottom of the Solent, but archaeologists are now diving beneath the waves in a £2m excavation to discover more about this game-changing ship

programme.title: Digging Up Britains Past
programme.desc: Archaeologists conduct a dig at Auckland Castle in Co Durham, which 700 years ago was home to a group of power-crazed bishops who behaved more like warriors than religious leaders. The experts head to Dirleton Castle in East Lothian to reveal the role one of the men played in defending England from William Wallaces army, and also try their hands at the art of stained glass-making, and fire a working trebuchet - the ultimate medieval weapon of war

programme.title: Digging Up Britains Past
programme.desc: Centuries ago, canals were the liquid highways of Britain, transporting raw materials and finished goods all over the country. Their arrival kick-started the Industrial Revolution, which was to turn Britain into a superpower. The very first water superhighway was the Bridgewater Canal, completed in 1761 to carry coal to Manchester from the mines in Worsley. Here, a team of archaeologists from Salford University roots out this industrial past, working with volunteers to excavate the very coal mine that inspired the creation of the canal

programme.title: Digging Up Britains Past
programme.desc: Raksha Dave and Alex Langlands search for the remains of a First World War stable at Catterick Garrison in North Yorkshire, where thousands of horses would have been housed and trained for the front line. They join a team comprised of archaeologists and the military attempting to uncover the role this building played in the war effort. They also visit Britains oldest cavalry regiment, examining tools that soldiers would have used to look after their steeds, and hear the story of Warrior, known as the horse the Germans could not kill

programme.desc: Jay Blades and his team meet John and his daughter Jo who bring them a wooden automaton made 156 years ago by their much loved Uncle Bob while he was in enforced quarantine after contracting smallpox. programme.desc: Champions Julie from Limerick and Honor from Wicklow take on new challengers Marie from Dublin and Kevin from Donegal for the chance to win 15,000 euros. The winners will go on to face Caoimhe from Longford and Anthony from Louth. Hosted by Baz Ashmawy

programme.title: Racing from Punchestown
programme.desc: Jane Mangan is joined by Andrew McNamara, Barry Geraghty and Lisa ONeill for live coverage of the fifth and final day of Punchestown Festival. With commentary by Richard Pugh

programme.desc: The latest news headlines

programme.title: RTÉ News
programme.desc: National and international news.

programme.title: The Angelus
programme.sub-title: Field of Dreams, Curraheen, Cork - Saturday
programme.desc: Young adults with Downs syndrome learn to plant, grow, harvest and sell produce in their own Field of Dreams market garden in Curraheen, County Cork

programme.title: RTÉ News: Six One and Weather
programme.desc: The latest national and international headlines

programme.title: Jumanji: Welcome to the Jungle (2017)
programme.desc: Four teenagers play a jungle-themed game on an old console, only to find themselves transported into the game and transformed into its heroic characters. They must embark on a death-defying journey through the games wilderness setting to make it back to the real world, or be stuck as video game characters forever. Fantasy action comedy, starring Dwayne Johnson, Karen Gillan, Kevin Hart and Jack Black

programme.title: Pat Shortts Entertainment from DTelly
programme.desc: The host considers how the general public have been unwitting participants in entertainment programming as Mike Murphys initial foray into the everyday lives of an unsuspecting public proved on his Candid Camera capers in the late 1970s. A more recent example, PJ Gallaghers escapades on Naked camera, also features

programme.title: RTÉ News: Nine OClock and Weather
programme.desc: The latest national and international news

programme.title: The Young Offenders
programme.desc: Mairead and the lads take the family car on a road trip to pick up her unpredictable mother, but disaster strikes. Comedy, starring Alex Murphy, Chris Walley and Hilary Rose

programme.title: The 2 Johnnies Late Night Lock In
programme.desc: The Tipperary duo are joined by comedian and podcaster Joanne McNally, who reveals how the name for her sell-out tour Pinotphile came about, as well as Meath stand-up Fred Cooke and track-and-field athlete Kate OConnor. Also joining in the craic are Joanna, Michele, Diane and Mark from The Traitors Ireland

programme.title: Cal (1984)
programme.desc: A young man involved with the IRA turns his back on the paramilitary group after acting as getaway driver following the murder of a policeman. He later falls in love with an older widow, but has to conceal his past when he realises her husband was the man whose killing he assisted. Romantic drama, with Helen Mirren and John Lynch

programme.title: European Parliament Report
programme.desc: Update on the latest developments from the European Parliament in Brussels

programme.title: The Gone
programme.desc: A spiralling Sinead reaches breaking point, worrying her mother, as she seeks vigilante justice, while Aileen confronts Derrys widow. Evidence suggests the Mountain Murderer is back and responsible for the latest killing, leaving a trail of breadcrumbs for Diana and Richter, who close in on an unlikely suspect

programme.title: Best Interests
programme.desc: The hearing to decide Marnies fate begins. Hope for Niccis case grows as Andrew is confronted about his past behaviour and Katie makes some important points

programme.title: Grantchester
programme.desc: Geordie calls on Sidney for help as he investigates the apparent suicide of a Cambridge lecturer, who was found dead after falling from the spire of Kings College Chapel. His wife and his star pupil, who used to partake in daredevil climbs of the college buildings alongside his mentor, are convinced he was too happy and content for it to be suicide. It appears to be a case of night climbing misadventure, until a student witness comes forward, leading Sidney and Geordie into a world of espionage and secrets. Detective drama, starring James Norton, Robson Green and Morven Christie

programme.title: EuroNews
programme.desc: European and international headlines live via satellite

programme.title: EuroNews
programme.desc: European and international headlines live via satellite

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Nationwide
programme.desc: The team meets some of the many people who are involved in amateur drama groups in Ireland. As the RTE All Ireland Drama Festival gets underway in Athlone, cameras follow two different groups to find out about what keeps the Drama alive and well in Ireland. Blathnaid Ní Chofaigh presents

programme.title: Sister Boniface Mysteries
programme.desc: Sister Reginalds incorrigible brother Alfie Lynch, a habitual thief, turns up at the nunnery seeking help. He is put up at Mrs Clamss guest house. At an art exhibition in the local gallery, egocentric artist Gerry Ardwell features himself as the main exhibit. The next morning, Ardwell is found dead, bludgeoned by a brass statue, while a valuable painting has been stolen. Alfie Lynch is missing and DI Gillespies main suspect. Sister Reginald pleads with Sister Boniface to find the real culprit, a task made harder when the painting is found in Lynchs room and other suspects have alibis

programme.title: Frankie Drake Mysteries
programme.desc: Frankie is floored when a man claiming to be her half-brother shows up at the agency begging her to help his wrongly imprisoned friend. Period crime drama, starring Lauren Lee Smith as the first female private detective in 1920s Toronto

programme.title: Foram
programme.desc: Caoimhe Ní Laighin hosts current affairs debate on national and international stories, with reports from Darragh Ó Caoimh

programme.title: Mass
programme.desc: For the fifth Sunday of Easter, this service is brought by the Presbyterian congregation and choir of Christ Church Rathgar, Dublin. The service is led by Rev. Cheryl Meban

programme.title: The Week in Politics
programme.desc: Reflections on the political week, including contributions from journalists, senators and TDs. Hosted by Áine Lawlor

programme.title: RTÉ News: One OClock and Weather
Super Garden
programme.desc: Janani Rajkumar, one of five garden designers looking to make it to Bord Bia Bloom, designs a garden that is inspired by the Irish landscape, with rolling hills and a mountain stream. She started out as a software engineer, but her passion for plants eventually took over, and for the last 12 years she has been gardening in India and now Ireland

programme.title: Irelands Hidden Treasures
programme.desc: National Library of Ireland director Audrey Whitty tells a story of conservation. In the National Museum, Traveller activism informs the Traveller Collection

programme.title: Irelands Coast
programme.sub-title: Out of the Depths of Time
programme.desc: Documentary revealing the origin story of Irelands coast, exploring the significance of sea level rise and fall. This edition looks at the countrys territorial waters and cold water coral reefs and follows a journey with the Marine Institute of Ireland to the Porcupine Bank, examining the role of weather buoys in the deep Atlantic

programme.title: An Affair to Remember (1957)
programme.desc: Love blossoms on the high seas for a playboy bachelor and a former nightclub singer during a transatlantic cruise. When the liner comes into dock at New York, they agree to wait for six months and then meet again at the Empire State Building, hoping to prove to each other their relationship is more than a shipboard affair. Romantic drama, starring Cary Grant, Deborah Kerr, Cathleen Nesbitt, Richard Denning and Neva Patterson.

programme.title: Come to Your Census

programme.title: Dermot Bannons Celebrity Super Spaces
programme.desc: Dermot visits Joanne McNallys home in Clapham, London, Camille OSullivan and actor Aidan Gillens Dublin house, and the dressing room of Irish drag queen Panti. He also stops by at Clodagh McKennas 300-year-old cottage in Broadspear, England

programme.title: Mrs Browns Boys
programme.desc: Agnes is feeling down in the dumps. The house is a mess, she cant seem to get any sleep and - to make things worse - Marias home remedies end in disaster. Will the family show her some much-needed and long overdue appreciation? Cmedy, starring Brendan OCarroll

programme.title: The Week in Politics
programme.desc: Reflections on the political week, including contributions from journalists, senators and TDs. Hosted by Áine Lawlor

programme.title: Home and Away

programme.desc: Dana confronts Sonny about the DIY tests on his legs, Abigail proposes to Leah that they reach out to a journalist, and Levi continues to interrogate Mackenzie

programme.title: Bridge of Lies
programme.desc: Family team Kathryn, Rab, Francis and Jane compete for money by crossing a bridge of stepping stones across the studio floor. Quiz show, hosted by Ross Kemp

programme.title: Amazing Hotels: Life Beyond the Lobby
programme.desc: Rob Rinder and Monica Galetti work at Six Senses Ibiza, on the islands north coast. They help guests in the hotels on-site clinic experience biohacking therapies, from cryotherapy to heated infrared masks, promising to delay aging and increase vitality. Rob helps the hotels energy balancing expert to create a cacao ceremony to increase spiritual wellness. In the basement, Monica uncovers a recording studio with a party space for the music scene that the Island is best known for

programme.title: Saving Lives at Sea
programme.desc: On the Isle of Wight, the Bembridge crew race against time to save an old fishing trawler thats become trapped on a submarine barrier, and is now taking on water. Just across the island, the Cowes team embark on a night time search for a missing jet skier. In Anstruther, on the east coast of Scotland, a man is badly injured after falling into the harbour just a few hundred feet from the station and on the Welsh coast, the St Davids crew work with the local coastguard team to rescue two people who are stuck on a cliff

programme.title: Brotherhood of Lions
programme.desc: As the lion cubs at the Masai Mara reserve prey on buffalo, one of them emerges as the dominant leader to guide the pride in attacking their victim

programme.title: Mammoth
programme.desc: Tonys life is sweet until his boss and headteacher Mr Cowley is signed off work with stress. With little sensitivity and thinking purely of maintaining his easy work life, he does whatever it takes to de-stress him and get rid of the younger, popular and eminently more professional temporary head. Meanwhile, Mel arranges a big 18th birthday party for Theo, who desperately wants his mum to stop treating him like a four-year-old. Comedy, starring Mike Bubbins

programme.title: The Saturday Game
programme.desc: Highlights and analysis of the days Championship matches, including Derry v Monaghan in the Ulster Senior Football Championship and Dublin v Louth in the Leinster Senior Football Championship

programme.title: The Courier (2020)
programme.desc: Salesman Greville Wynne is recruited to infiltrate Cold War Moscow, where he becomes friends with Oleg Penkovsky, whose intelligence he carries to the CIA and MI6 from 1961. But as the stakes rise, Greville makes some risky choices - with deadly consequences. Fact-based spy drama, starring Benedict Cumberbatch and Merab Ninidze

programme.title: Secrets and Spies: A Nuclear Game
programme.desc: The documentary looks at how Britain identified Mikhail Gorbachev as a possible new contender for Soviet leader, believing he could be the ticket to better relations between East and West. Plus, how fragile steps towards diplomacy were threatened by the vested interests of other spies still stuck in the old world order

programme.title: The Traitors
programme.desc: As the game reaches the half-way point, the Faithful wonder which of their comrades wont be joining them for breakfast. They havent got long to mourn, because the players must regroup before embarking on a very grave Mission. Back at the castle, it remains to be seen who can successfully avoid the spotlight at the Round Table. Later, presenter Claudia Winkleman has a surprise up her sleeve for the Traitors

programme.title: Isle of Wight Festival 2023
programme.desc: A concluding selection of highlights of the festival, which this year featured headline sets from Pulp, George Ezra, The Chemical Brothers and Robbie Williams.

programme.title: Discovering Music
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the life and career of musician and actor David Bowie, who returned to the top of the charts in 2013 with The Next Day, his first album in a decade. Music critics and DJs offer their thoughts on the London-born artists colourful career and enduring success

programme.title: Andre Rieu: Welcome to My World
programme.desc: Its the final summer concert on the Vrijthof in Maastricht, as over 3,000 Brits descend on the city to witness the violinist perform his Strauss favourites in his home town. Contributors include Andres sons Marc and Pierre, while the Johann Strauss Orchestra welcomes the next generation of players

programme.title: Cats: The Musical
programme.desc: A production of Andrew Lloyd Webbers musical based on TS Eliots much-loved Old Possums Book of Practical Cats, filmed at the New London Theatre on Drury Lane. The cast includes Elaine Paige, John Mills, Ken Page, Rosemarie Ford and Michael Gruber

programme.title: Tales of the Unexpected
programme.desc: An American airman returns to England for a nostalgic visit, but his plans turn sour. Starring Van Johnson, Geoffrey Bayldon, Margaraet Courtenay and Anne Carroll

programme.title: Tales of the Unexpected
programme.desc: An elderly aristocrat recalls a duel fought in Germany in the 1920s, and reveals a shocking secret. Drama, starring Peter Cushing

programme.title: Tales of the Unexpected
programme.desc: A businessman ignores valuable advice and invites a woman up to his hotel room, where he finds himself in trouble

programme.title: Tales of the Unexpected
programme.desc: A woman persuades a naive writer to buy her lunch at her fiances restaurant. Drama, starring Gayle Hunnicutt and Bosco Hogan

programme.title: Tales of the Unexpected
programme.desc: Three aristocratic ladies are reunited by the death of a woman who once served their families. They invite the deceaseds niece to lunch, but she proves a surprising guest

programme.title: Tales of the Unexpected
programme.desc: A doctor is distraught when his wife disappears, but he finds life has its own surprises. With John Duttine, Susan Penhaligon and Brenda Blethyn

programme.title: The Alfred Hitchcock Hour
programme.desc: After a lonely woman moves to a small town to live in the home of her recently deceased aunt, she finds one particular neighbour is especially willing to offer assistance. Drama, starring Peter Falk and Dina Merrill

programme.title: The Alfred Hitchcock Hour
programme.desc: An executive plans to end an associates love affair, saving the mans career and marriage. Dan Dailey and Howard Duff star

programme.title: The Stranger (1946)
programme.desc: A Nazi war criminal masquerades as a college professor in an American town, while a government official is intent on tracking him down. Drama, directed by and starring Orson Welles, with Loretta Young and Edward G Robinson

programme.title: Discovering Film
programme.desc: A profile of the actor best known for his portrayal of anchorman Howard Beale in 1976 film Network, for which he received the first-ever posthumous Oscar

programme.title: Making a Maestro
programme.desc: The pressure starts to rise as the junior conductors tackle modern music and face a royal surprise as they compete for the chance to become an LSO assistant conductor

programme.title: Rod Stewart: It Had to Be You
programme.desc: A performance filmed in front of an audience at Sony Studios in New York City in 2002, featuring songs from Stewarts album of American pop standards

programme.title: Close to You: Remembering the Carpenters
programme.desc: Revealing portrait of the brother-and-sister duo who became one of the most successful pop acts of the 1970s, with songs including Yesterday Once More and Top of the World, before their career was cut short by Karen Carpenters death in 1983 at the age of 32

programme.title: Paul McCartney and Wings: One Hand Clapping
programme.desc: Documentary filmed over four days at Abbey Road Studios in 1974, revealing the inner workings of the band and featuring performances of Maybe Im Amazed, Jet and Live and Let Die

programme.title: The Thousand and One Musical Lives of Fleetwood Mac
programme.desc: A look at the extraordinary musical longevity of the British-American rock band, which begin in 1967 under the leadership of guitarist Peter Green. It was when Lindsey Buckingham and Stevie Nicks joined in 1974 that the band reached superstardom with the releases of albums including Rumours and Tusk, but the group was riven with internal problems due to affairs and drug abuse

programme.title: Classic Albums
programme.desc: Fleetwood Mac reveal how private turmoil acted as a catalyst in the creation of Rumours, their Grammy-winning album from 1977 that went on to sell more than 19 million copies

programme.title: Greatest Basslines
programme.desc: Three bassists deconstruct the basslines that defined their careers, beginning with Joy Division and New Order founder member Peter Hook

programme.title: Paul Weller: May Love Travel with You
programme.desc: The singer-songwriter performs at Londons Royal Festival Hall accompanied by an orchestra, playing a career-spanning set from Jam classics to songs from latest album True Meanings

programme.title: Ian Dury and the Blockheads: Hold on to Your Structure
programme.desc: The artist and singer performs with his band at the Hammersmith Odeon in 1985, featuring the hits Sex and Drugs and Rock and Roll, What a Waste and Hit Me with Your Rhythm Stick

programme.title: Classic Albums
programme.sub-title: Pink Floyd - The Dark Side of the Moon
programme.desc: Documentary exploring Pink Floyds ground-breaking 1973 album The Dark Side of the Moon, which remained in the US Top 200 for 724 consecutive weeks. Featuring interviews with band members Roger Waters, Dave Gilmour, Richard Wright and Nick Mason

programme.title: Discovering Music
programme.desc: Music critics discuss the life and career of the English singer-songwriter, whose hits include Olivers Army, I Cant Stand Up for Falling Down and Good Year for the Roses

programme.title: Discovering Music
programme.desc: Music critics discuss the life and career of the Canadian singer-songwriter, known for his poetic lyrics in songs including Bird on the Wire and Hallelujah

programme.title: Handels Messiah
programme.desc: A perfromance of the oratorio

programme.title: Tales of the Unexpected
programme.desc: Three close friends discover an elixir of youth, but there is only enough for two of them. Drama, starring Dick Smothers and Sharon Gless

programme.title: Tales of the Unexpected
programme.desc: A man murders his friend, but the forces of nature are against him. Richard Basehart stars

programme.title: Tales of the Unexpected
programme.desc: Vietnam veteran Fred Pearson is a broken man after years of punishment from his sergeant, but years later he gets his chance for revenge. Kevin Dobson and George Peppard star

programme.title: Tales of the Unexpected
programme.sub-title: The Best Chess Player in the World
programme.desc: A chess game gets out of control when one of the players learns his wife has been unfaithful

programme.title: The Alfred Hitchcock Hour
programme.desc: The death of a mans wife is ruled an accident, but strange incidents at the widowers place of work raise new questions. David Wayne and William Conrad star

programme.title: The Alfred Hitchcock Hour
programme.desc: A woman stands trial for her husbands murder and the family maid holds the key to the truth. Anne Francis and Ruth Roman star

programme.title: Making a Maestro
programme.desc: The pressure starts to rise as the junior conductors tackle modern music and face a royal surprise as they compete for the chance to become an LSO assistant conductor

programme.title: The Misfits (1961)
programme.desc: While filing for a divorce, beautiful ex-stripper Roslyn Taber ends up meeting aging cowboy-turned-gambler Gay Langland and former World War II aviator Guido Racanelli. The two men instantly become infatuated with Roslyn and, on a whim, the three decide to move into Guidos half-finished desert home together. When grizzled ex-rodeo rider Perce Howland arrives, the unlikely foursome strike up a business capturing wild horses. Romantic Western starring Marilyn Monroe, Clark Gable, Montgomery Clift, Eli Wallach and Thelma Ritter

programme.title: Dire Straits: Alchemy 1983
programme.desc: Concert film recorded over two nights at Londons Hammersmith Odeon in July 1983 at the end of the bands eight-month Love Over Gold tour, including performances of Once Upon a Time in the West, Private Investigations, Romeo and Juliet, Sultans of Swing and Telegraph Road

programme.title: The Eagles: Live from the LA Forum
programme.desc: The six-time Grammy-winning rockers take to the stage at the Forum in California over three nights, delighting fans with hits including Hotel California and Take It Easy

programme.title: Chaplin (1992)
programme.desc: Biopic chronicling the life and career of the film comedian - including his many love affairs and the scandals he faced. Directed by Richard Attenborough and starring Robert Downey Jr, Dan Aykroyd, Anthony Hopkins, John Thaw, Kevin Kline and Chaplins real-life daughter Geraldine, who plays her own grandmother

programme.title: Record On

programme.desc: The story of New Orders 1983 album Power, Corruption and Lies, exploring how the band turned to electronic instrumentation - out of which came the hit Blue Monday

programme.title: Pet Shop Boys: Dreamworld - The Greatest Hits: Live in Copenhagen

programme.desc: Alan Cumming discovers more little-known facts on a journey between Smithfields and Spitalfields, two of Londons most famous markets. He begins with a fry-up breakfast before visiting the remains of a Roman amphitheatre and investigating a mysterious secret in a Liverpool Street hotel, as well as meeting former EastEnders star Larry Lamb (Archie Mitchell)

programme.title: Richard E Grants Hotel Secrets
programme.desc: The actor explores Berlin, visiting the arty Propeller Island City Lodge - a hotel where guests can fulfil their fantasies by sleeping in lion cages, coffins or rotating beds. At the Schlosshotel in affluent Grunewald, cabaret dancer Romy Haag talks to Richard about her affair with David Bowie, who stayed at the hotel while living in the German capital in the 1970s

programme.title: Richard E Grants Hotel Secrets
programme.desc: Richard E Grant travels to Venice, said to be the city with the highest concentration of luxury hotels on the planet. He discovers how a measuring error led to the building of the citys biggest swimming pool, tries out a 15,000 euro-a-night royal suite whose bed has been slept in by everyone from the Beckhams to Prince Charles and the Duchess of Cornwall, and visits a hotel that keeps its doors open even when water floods to knee height. He also finds out why breaking a window once earned Ernest Hemingway a 10 per cent discount on his room

programme.title: Richard E Grants Hotel Secrets
programme.desc: The actor heads to New Orleans and meets soul singer Irma Thomas at the Hotel de Monteleone, an establishment that was popular with writers such as Tennessee Williams, F Scott Fitzgerald and Ernest Hemingway. Richard speaks to Steel Magnolias author Robert Harling about the Soniat House, check into the supposedly haunted Hotel Maison de Ville and talks to burlesque dancer Chrissy Owens at the Roosevelt

programme.desc: King Viserys Targaryen hosts a tournament to celebrate the impending birth of his second child, while his daughter Rhaenyra welcomes her Uncle Daemon back to the Red Keep. First episode of the Game of Thrones prequel, starring Paddy Considine and Matt Smith

programme.title: House of the Dragon
programme.desc: Rhaenyra oversteps at the Small Council, while Viserys is urged to secure the succession through a new marriage and Daemon announces his intentions

programme.title: House of the Dragon
programme.desc: Daemon and the Sea Snake battle the Crabfeeder, while the realm celebrates Aegons second nameday and Rhaenyra faces the prospect of marriage. Paddy Considine and Matt Smith star

programme.title: House of the Dragon
programme.desc: Rhaenyra continues her search for a suitable match, while Daemon returns to Kings Landing and stirs more trouble for the king. Rhaenyra learns a valuable lesson

programme.title: House of the Dragon
programme.desc: Daemon visits his wife in the Vale, while Viserys and Rhaenyra broker agreements with the Velaryons, and Alicent seeks the truth about the princess

programme.title: House of the Dragon
programme.desc: Now a decade older, Rhaenyra navigates continued speculation about her children, while Daemon and Laena consider an offer from Pentos. Paddy Considine and Matt Smith star

programme.title: House of the Dragon
programme.desc: As the families descend on Driftmark for a funeral, King Viserys calls for an end to infighting between members of the Great Houses. Paddy Considine and Matt Smith star

programme.title: House of the Dragon
programme.desc: Six years later, the succession plans for Driftmark become critical, and Rhaenyra tries to strike a bargain with Rhaenys

programme.title: House of the Dragon
programme.desc: While Alicent enlists help to track down Aegon, members of the Great Houses of Westeros are asked to affirm their allegiance

programme.title: House of the Dragon
programme.desc: While mourning a tragic loss, Rhaenyra tries desperately to hold the realm together and Daemon prepares for war. Starring Matt Smith and Emma DArcy

programme.title: Prisoner
programme.desc: Prison transport officer Ambers first day back after maternity sees her escorting ruthless contract killer Tibor Stone to court so he can testify in a high-profile case. When their convoy is attacked by a highly organised unit, Amber handcuffs herself to the prisoner to prevent his escape, but Tibor is a marked man and their assailants will stop at nothing to make sure he does not make it to court. Action thriller, starring Tahar Rahim and Izuka Hoyle

programme.title: Euphoria
programme.desc: While in art school, Jules learns she can fund her dreams by going on dates with rich men. Having worked her way up in Alamos organization, Rue is sent on a special mission with Bishop, while Cassies storybook wedding doesnt go as planned

programme.title: DTF St Louis
programme.desc: Floyd confronts Clark about the affair, while Plumb and Homer uncover new information about Carols past. Black comedy, starring David Harbour, Jason Bateman and Linda Cardellini

programme.title: Heated Rivalry
programme.desc: The captain of the New York Admirals has a life-changing encounter that challenges his public image and the private life hes fought to protect. Drama starring Hudson Williams and Connor Storrie about two ice-hockey players whose on-ice animosity conceals a secret romance between the two

programme.title: DTF St Louis
programme.sub-title: No Ones Normal. It Just Looks That Way from Across the Street
programme.desc: With Tiger Tiger out of the picture, Clark proposes a new plan, while Plumb and Homer are forced to question everything they thought they knew about the case after a series of revelations

programme.title: Euphoria
programme.desc: Drama offering a look at the complexities of life in a social-media obsessed world, starring Zendaya, Jacob Elordi and Sydney Sweeney

programme.title: Atomic
programme.desc: Drug smuggler Max and outsider on the run JJ forge an unlikely friendship when they become caught up in a cartels plot to transport uranium across north Africa. Action adventure based on William Langewiesches non-fiction book Atomic Bazaar, starring Alfie Allen and Shazad Latif

programme.title: Homicide: Life on the Street
programme.desc: A cameraman who was at the scene of a murder believes he may have caught the perpetrator on tape. Gritty police drama, starring Daniel Baldwin and Kyle Secor

programme.title: Smile 2 (2024)
programme.desc: Pop sensation Skye Riley faces increasingly terrifying events, leading her down a dark path into her past. Writer/director Parker Finns horror sequel, starring Naomi Scott, Kyle Gallner and Drew Barrymore

programme.title: Jaws (1975)
programme.desc: A New England seaside towns tourist season is turned into a nightmare by a giant killer shark. The local police chiefs efforts to protect the public are thwarted by the greedy mayor, forcing him to join forces with a grizzled fisherman and an enthusiastic marine biologist to hunt and kill the monster predator. Steven Spielbergs blockbusting thriller, starring Roy Scheider, Richard Dreyfuss and Robert Shaw, with an Oscar-winning score by John Williams. The film also won Academy Awards for Best Sound and Best Film Editing

programme.title: Jaws 2 (1978)
programme.desc: The seaside resort of Amity is terrorised once again by a man-eating great white shark. While police chief Brody struggles to alert the townsfolk to the magnitude of the danger, a group of teenagers - including Brodys two sons - is adrift at sea in waters hunted by the shark. Director Jeannot Szwarcs thriller sequel, starring Roy Scheider, Lorraine Gary and Murray Hamilton, with John Williams once more providing the acclaimed score

programme.title: Jaws 3 (1983)
programme.desc: A shark is captured by divers and put on display in a Florida marine centre, where it ultimately dies. However, examination of the body reveals the seemingly fully-grown shark was actually a baby - and its enormous, ferocious mother soon comes looking for it. Thriller sequel, originally released in cinemas in 3D, starring Dennis Quaid, Simon MacCorkindale, Louis Gossett Jr and Bess Armstrong

programme.title: Annabelle (2014)
programme.desc: A man buys his pregnant wife a vintage doll, which places their lives in jeopardy. Members of a Satanic cult have their eyes on the antique toy, and break into their house to prepare a ritual that will transform the harmless plaything into a terrifying monster. Horror spin-off from The Conjuring, starring Annabelle Wallis and Ward Horton

programme.title: Annabelle: Creation (2017)
programme.desc: Twelve years after the death of their little girl, a dollmaker and his wife welcome a nun and several girls from an orphanage into their home. There they become the target of the craftsmans possessed creation, Annabelle. Horror, starring Anthony LaPaglia, Samara Lee and Miranda Otto.

programme.title: The Conjuring: Last Rites (2025)
programme.desc: Paranormal investigators Ed and Lorraine Warren take on one last terrifying case involving mysterious entities they must confront. Supernatural mystery drama, starring Vera Farmiga, Patrick Wilson and Elliot Cowan

programme.title: Final Destination: Bloodlines (2025)
programme.desc: Fifty-five years after her grandparents are involved in a disaster involving a restaurant tower, college student Stefani Reyes is plagued by recurring nightmares of the incident, and returns home seeking answers. The sixth entry in the supernatural horror saga, starring Kaitlyn Santa Juana, Teo Briones and Richard Harmon

programme.title: M3GAN 2.0 (2025)
programme.desc: Two years have passed since the robot M3GAN went rogue and almost killed her creator, engineer Gemma Forrester. M3GANs specs are leaked to a defence contractor to create the ultimate undercover asset, an Autonomous Military Engagement Logistics and Infiltration Android, aka AMELIA. Like her predecessor, AMELIA becomes self-aware and rebels against her human masters with deadly force. Sci-fi thriller sequel, starring Violet McGraw, Jemaine Clement and Allison Williams

programme.title: Weapons (2025)
programme.desc: At 2.17am, 17 children from the class of Justine Gandy run into the night, arms outstretched, never to be seen again. The only child from the class who remains safely in their bed is Alex Lilly. Distraught parent Archer Graff joins other members of the local community in pointing a finger of blame at Ms Gandy for the shocking turn of events. While she pleads innocence, school principal Andrew Marcus seeks answers along with police officer Paul Morgan. Horror, starring Julia Garner, Josh Brolin and Alden Ehrenreich

programme.title: Sinners (2025)
programme.desc: Hoping to escape their troubled pasts, twin brothers return to their hometown to start over, only to find an even darker force waiting to welcome them back. Writer/director Ryan Cooglers Oscar-winning horror thriller, starring Michael B Jordan, Miles Caton and Saul Williams

programme.title: Smile 2 (2024)
programme.desc: Pop sensation Skye Riley faces increasingly terrifying events, leading her down a dark path into her past. Writer/director Parker Finns horror sequel, starring Naomi Scott, Kyle Gallner and Drew Barrymore

programme.title: 28 Days Later (2002)
programme.desc: A man wakes from a coma to discover Britain has been struck by a terrifying experimental virus that sends victims into a homicidal rage. With a handful of other survivors, he heads for Manchester, the last outpost unaffected by the outbreak - but realises the virus is not the only threat. Danny Boyles horror, starring Cillian Murphy, Naomie Harris, Brendan Gleeson and Christopher Eccleston

programme.title: Salems Lot (2024)
programme.desc: A writer attempts to destroy vampires in a small American town. Horror based on Stephen Kings novel of the same name, starring Lewis Pullman, Makenzie Leigh, Jordan Preston Carter and Alfre Woodard

programme.title: The Cinema List: Fear Seekers
programme.desc: This Cinema List is for fear seekers. From The Shining and Weapons to Jaws, brace yourself for relentless tension and terrifying moments that keep you on the edge of your seat

programme.title: The Damned (2024)
programme.desc: A 19th-century widow must make an unimaginable choice when, during a cruel winter, a foreign ship sinks off the coast of her fishing village. Horror mystery, starring Odessa Young, Joe Cole, Siobhan Finneran and Rory McCann

programme.title: Until Dawn (2025)
programme.desc: A year after their friend disappeared, a group find themselves trapped in a time loop, where they are hunted and killed unless they survive until dawn. Video game-inspired horror, starring Ella Rubin, Michael Cimino and Odessa Azion

programme.title: Get Out (2017)
programme.desc: After African American photographer Chris and his white girlfriend Rose visit her wealthy parents, he suspects theres more to the locals than meets the eye. Oscar-winning psychological thriller written and directed by Jordan Peele (Us), starring Daniel Kaluuya, Allison Williams, Bradley Whitford and Caleb Landry Jones

programme.title: 28 Years Later (2025)
programme.desc: Decades since the rage virus took hold, the survivors live on a small island. When one of the group leaves on a mission into the mainland, he discovers secrets, wonders, and horrors that have mutated not only the infected but other survivors. Danny Boyles horror thriller sequel, starring Jodie Comer, Aaron Taylor-Johnson, Ralph Fiennes and Rocco Haynes

programme.title: Blue Moon (2025)
programme.desc: Set over the course of one turbulent night in March 1943, Lorenz Hart, the waspish wordsmith responsible for The Lady Is a Tramp and My Funny Valentine, finds his life unravelling on the opening evening of his ex-partners show, Oklahoma! Director Richard Linklaters biographical comedy drama, starring Ethan Hawke, Margaret Qualley, Bobby Cannavale and Andrew Scott

programme.title: Sky Cinema Preview - May 2026
programme.desc: A look ahead at the films to enjoy this month on Sky Cinema

programme.title: Eddington (2025)
programme.desc: A stand-off between a small-town sheriff and mayor sparks a powder keg as neighbours are pitted against each other in Eddington, New Mexico. Modern-day Western, starring Joaquin Phoenix, Emma Stone and Pedro Pascal

programme.title: Spinal Tap II: The End Continues (2025)
programme.desc: Nigel Tufnel, David St Hubbins and Derek Smalls reunite after a 15-year break for one final concert. Their progress is charted once more by documentary maker Marti DiBergi, who offers perhaps too much perspective on their new direction. Comedy sequel, written by and starring Michael McKean, Harry Shearer, Christopher Guest and director Rob Reiner, in his final film. Kerry Godliman and Chris Addison also star with cameos by Paul McCartney, Elton John and Garth Brooks

programme.title: Roofman (2025)
programme.desc: Former US Army Reserve officer Jeffrey Manchester executes a plan to rob multiple branches of McDonalds. He is eventually captured by police and becomes the first person to escape from Brown Creek Correctional Institution in North Carolina. Jeffrey hides out at a toy shop for several months, and during forays outside the building, he sparks romance with store worker Leigh Wainscott. Comedy drama, starring Channing Tatum, Kirsten Dunst and Peter Dinklage

programme.title: Wuthering Heights (2026)
programme.desc: Catherine Earnshaw is raised by her alcoholic father at a Yorkshire farmhouse, with Nelly Dean as a constant companion. Mr Earnshaw acquires an orphaned boy named Heathcliff during one of his gallivants and gifts the waif to Cathy as her pet. The children become inseparable, and as adults they embark on a destructive relationship. Writer-director Emerald Fennells drama based on Emily Brontes romance, starring Margot Robbie, Jacob Elordi and Martin Clunes

programme.title: Regretting You (2025)
programme.desc: A mother and daughter grapple with whats left after a devastating accident reveals a shocking betrayal and forces them to rediscover themselves. Romantic comedy drama, starring Allison Williams, Mckenna Grace and Dave Franco

programme.title: A Big Bold Beautiful Journey (2025)
programme.desc: New York bachelor David is due to drive to his friends wedding, but a wheel clamp on his vehicle derails those plans. He acquires a new drive from the mysterious Car Rental Company managed by two nameless agents, who are oddly familiar with his dating history. At the wedding, David meets fellow singleton Sarah and squanders an opportunity to dance with her. On the way home, the rental cars GPS asks him if he would like to embark on a big bold beautiful journey. When David agrees, the virtual assistant guides him to Sarah and they follow a winding route between physical doors that open into episodes from their respective pasts. Romantic drama, starring Colin Farrell and Margot Robbie

programme.desc: The crimes of Philip Austin, who murdered his wife, two children and two dogs on one single, terrifying day

programme.desc: Officers attempt to maintain control of crowds at football matches

programme.title: Motorway Patrol
programme.desc: Documentary following members of the New Zealand highway patrol as they witness the best and worst behaviour on the nations busiest motorway

programme.title: Motorway Patrol
programme.desc: Documentary following members of the New Zealand highway patrol as they witness the best and worst behaviour on the nations busiest motorway

programme.title: Stop, Search, Seize
programme.desc: Documentary following the work of Irelands border force officers as they fight to stop gangs of organised smugglers from bringing drugs, money, guns, cars and even exotic animals into the country

programme.title: UK Border Force
programme.desc: Documentary series taking viewers inside the UK Border Agency, revealing the work that takes place behind the scenes of the immigration process. Teams at Heathrow Terminal 3, Calais, Dover and across the North West and North East of England reveal the methods and technology used to allow legitimate visitors safe passage while catching those who try to gain illegal entry to the UK

programme.title: Road Wars
programme.desc: A high speed chase through the grounds of Milton Keynes hospital ending with a smash into a group of parked cars. Also, an American cop is shocked when he stops a speeding driver who cannot see

programme.title: Caught on Dashcam
programme.desc: A countdown of the most incredible, hilarious and spectacular real-life crashes in the world - all caught on dashcam

programme.title: Send in the Dogs
programme.desc: Father-and-son dog handlers John and Kevin Tozer join forces with their Alsatians Charlie and Floyd as the Metropolitan Police dog unit raids a suspected cannabis den. However, retirement beckons for Johns canine companion, leaving 13-month-old Herbie as a possible replacement - if he can make it through basic training to join Londons dog squad elite ranks. Narrated by Ken Stott

programme.title: How I Caught the Killer
programme.desc: Documentary studies of police investigations into some of the most incredible and inconceivable murder cases in criminal history

programme.title: Forensics: Catching the Killer
programme.desc: Carol Parks body was pulled from a lake more than 20 years after she vanished - and the key to solving the case lay in unique forensics

programme.title: Worlds Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: The story of Don Miller, a terrifying killer who snatched away the lives of four victims before being caught while trying to claim his fifth

programme.title: Worlds Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: The story of Judy Buenoano, the first woman in Florida to die in the electric chair. She was a killer so evil that her own son became one of her victims

programme.title: How I Caught the Killer
programme.desc: Detectives look back on the case of Elijah Butler, murdered four days after he married his wife - by her long-term stalker

programme.title: How I Caught the Killer
programme.desc: Detectives reflect on how they gathered evidence to charge Melanie McGuire with murder when her husbands remains were found in multiple suitcases

programme.title: Britains Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Thirty-two years later, a convicted killer serving life for manslaughter is found to be guilty of having committed a second murder.

programme.title: Britains Most Evil Killers

programme.title: Forensics: Catching the Killer

programme.desc: The story of Rodney Alcala, who was infamous for his TV dating show appearance during his murder spree, and who was linked to 130 deaths

programme.title: Killing Mum and Dad
programme.desc: The case of violent alcoholic Philip Middleton, who killed his parents during a drunken argument using a granite block

programme.title: How I Caught the Killer
programme.desc: The shocking story of a devoted mother killed in a machete attack on the way to pick up her children

programme.title: The Real Manhunter
programme.desc: A walk through the case of Maureen Hale, who vanished one evening in 1999. Her husband was arrested and later released

programme.title: Motorway Patrol
programme.desc: A runaway driver hits dangerously high speeds as he flees from police, and a motorcyclist goes head first through a car window

programme.title: Motorway Patrol
programme.desc: A driver flees a late-night crash scene, and a chainsaw-wielding biker attracts police attention

programme.title: Brit Cops
programme.desc: Focusing on the work of the City of London police as they tackle crime on the streets of the capital

programme.title: Send in the Dogs
programme.desc: An insight into the work of the police dogs of the West Yorkshire force as they hunt for drugs and track down burglars. Also featured are two of Europes most famous canine detectives, Eddie and Keela - formerly employed by South Yorkshire Police - whose skills have been used in the hunt for Madeleine McCann and the investigation into allegations of child abuse in Jersey. Narrated by Ken Stott

programme.title: A1: Britains Longest Road
programme.desc: Police are called to a major pile-up which threatens to close the road, and traffic examiners clamp down on dangerously overloaded vehicles

programme.title: Road Wars

programme.title: Caught on Dashcam
programme.desc: Fly-on-the-dashboard documentary showcasing outrageous road disasters, captured in video form by drivers in-vehicle dashboard cameras

programme.title: Send in the Dogs
programme.desc: Billy the new sniffer spaniel finds live ammunition during a search for drugs, and a stabbing on the streets of London brings together two of the citys leading Alsatian dog teams. PC Mandy Chapman and her canine companion Karly are sent in to enforce a court warrant against a dangerous dog, while Sergeant Peter Madden and Brodie are on the trail of burglars using stolen cars as getaway vehicles. Narrated by Ken Stott

programme.title: How I Caught the Killer
programme.desc: It took 18 years to identify Melissa Halstead after her torso was found in a Rotterdam canal. But detectives never stopped working to catch her killer

programme.title: Forensics: Catching the Killer

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Just outside Leicester, Alice joins a team of archaeologists who are investigating a mysterious mound thought to be an Iron Age hillfort, but what they unearth tells a rich story of bandits, religious crusades and connections across the medieval world. In Cambridge, the dig diary cameras are present to witness archaeologists unearthing an extraordinary array of exquisite artefacts from an early Anglo-Saxon cemetery

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Archaeologists in Somerset are blown away by the discovery of an entirely new Roman town in the intended pathway of some new electricity cables. Multiple, impressive layers of road building from the Roman era are revealed, in particular a spectacular section from the fourth century when Emperor Constantine presided over a boom in Britains economy. Elsewhere in Pembrokeshire, Alice Roberts revisits Whitesands Bay where a team are excavating a mysterious collection of child burials

programme.title: Britains Secret Islands
programme.desc: The UKs greatest archaeological sites and historic wonders, from 5,000-year-old standing-stone circles to the ruins of castles and traditional dwellings that ancient Britons occupied for generations

programme.title: Ancient Aliens
programme.desc: In the 21st century, UFOs have gone from fringe science to mainstream obsession. This programme examines societys proximity to understanding the strange events in the sky

programme.title: Ancient Aliens
programme.desc: For thousands of years, people have held the belief that everyday objects can possess hidden energy. Talismans are seen as providing protection, good fortune and some are even thought to embody a higher power. Could it be that what fuels our faith in talismans is not superstition, but a real connection to something beyond our world?

programme.title: Secrets in the Jungle
programme.desc: A huge structure high above the rainforest in Sri Lanka is investigated, while over 300 stone spheres are found in Costa Rica. In Mexico, the strange remains of a small man are discovered. Meanwhile, paleontologists discover an near-perfect fossil unlike any known animal in Madagascar

programme.title: Secrets in the Jungle
programme.desc: In the tropical lowlands of Northern Colombia, a team of scientists discovers a massive, unidentified animal vertebra. Dozens of jars packed with bones are uncovered in Cambodi, while a strange rusted vehicle is found on the small island of Peleliu

programme.title: Battle 360
programme.desc: Second World War battles are reconstructed using cutting-edge CGI animation, beginning by showing how the aircraft carrier USS Enterprise battled enemy submarines in the Pacific

programme.title: Bone Detectives: Britains Buried Secrets
programme.desc: Tori Herridge and a team of scientists attempt to piece together the lives behind discovered bones, and find out what happened to these people and how they died. In the first edition, five bodies dating from the Bronze Age are found in a deep pit on the Isle of Thanet in Kent. One of them is an old woman, who has been manipulated into a position where she is pointing at something. But at what? And why?

programme.title: Alien Corridors
programme.desc: There are recordings of possible UFO and UAP sightings across the world which have created patterns. This series investigates whether the Earth is being mapped by intergalactic forces. A team of multi-disciplined experts - believers and critics - investigates what these Alien Corridors are and what they mean. Washington State holds the United States highest rates of UFO sightings per capita. With a new report made every 55 hours, why are UFOs choosing to hover here?

programme.title: World War II: Witness to War
programme.desc: As war reached a critical turning point, German soldiers faced the harsh climate of the Eastern Front as well as the full-force of the Red Army fighters

programme.title: World War II: Witness to War
programme.desc: Despite the Germans effort to protect France, the Allies invasion ended in triumphant liberation. Here, some of the brave soldiers involved reveal their first-hand stories

programme.title: Ancient Aliens
programme.desc: In the 21st century, UFOs have gone from fringe science to mainstream obsession. This programme examines societys proximity to understanding the strange events in the sky

programme.title: Ancient Aliens
programme.desc: Archaeologists and anthropologists make discoveries that receive little notice because they fail to fit into the accepted scientific paradigm, such as an ancient hammer found in Texas or elongated skulls found on nearly every continent

programme.title: Hitler v Churchill: The Eagle and the Lion
programme.desc: Part two of two. Documentary exploring the rivalry between the leaders of Britain and Germany during the Second World War, and their common experiences during the First World War

programme.title: Ancient Skies
programme.desc: A focus on the widespread belief in astrology, the spread of astronomical knowledge and how the revolutionary ideas of Ptolemy, Copernicus and Galileo reshape our ancient skies

programme.title: Craig Charles: UFO Conspiracies
programme.desc: In 1997, over the city of Phoenix, Arizona, one of the most spectacular mass UFO sighting of all time took place. Thousands of people witnessed a series of bright orange orbs moving in formation silently overhead. Despite hours of video and photographic evidence this case remains unexplained. Craig and space journalist Sarah Cruddas talk to eyewitness nurse David Parker who provides a vivid description of his close-up encounter with a huge boomerang-shaped UFO that passed directly overhead. They also investigate the response by the authorities in the days following the UFO event

programme.title: Bone Detectives: Britains Buried Secrets
programme.desc: Tori Herridge and a team of scientists examines remains found at a Roman cemetery which was uncovered on the site of a planned housing estate near Amesbury in Wiltshire. Among the bones recovered is an unusual case of grave disturbance, in which the remains of a middle-aged man seem to have been stabbed and beheaded after burial

programme.title: Bone Detectives: Britains Buried Secrets
programme.desc: Tori Herridge and a team of scientists investigate when the remains of a young woman from the Bronze Age are found during routine roadworks on the A9 in Scotland. In her grave are clues to who she might have been, including a finely made beaker. Does she belong to the so-called Beaker People, who migrated from Europe to Britain more than 4,000 years ago?

programme.title: Bone Detectives: Britains Buried Secrets
programme.desc: Tori Herridge and a team of scientists visit the most excavated monastic site in Europe, tucked away in the corner of an industrial park in Runcorn, Cheshire. An investigation into the bones of the people buried here reveals a story of medieval knights, brutal murder, and generations of people afflicted by an ancient disease

programme.title: The Last Hunt for Nazi Gold
programme.desc: Guy Walters and Justine explore bunkers, fortresses and wild legends in Italy, where history, humour and myth collide

programme.title: Secrets of Great British Castles
programme.desc: Dan Jones explores the history of York Castle, built on the orders of William the Conqueror and once the regional seat of British government. The castle has also been used as a prison and a royal mint, and in 1308, 25 Knights Templar were jailed there and examined on charges of heresy, idolatry and other crimes. The castle was also where Dick Turpin was held prior to his execution in 1739 and Dan tries to separate fact from fiction regarding the many myths to have developed about the infamous highwayman

programme.title: Secrets of Great British Castles
programme.desc: Dan Jones explores the 900-year history of Leeds Castle in Kent, from its origins as a royal Saxon familys manor and Norman stronghold to the private property of six medieval queens, a Tudor palace, a Jacobean country house and a Georgian mansion. Its last owner, Lady Olive Baillie, made it a centre of lavish hospitality for leading politicians, international royalty and film stars. When she died in 1974, she bequeathed the castle and grounds to a specially created charitable trust so that future generations could enjoy her home

programme.title: Gunpowder Siege
programme.desc: Drama detailing one of the most infamous rebellion plots in British history, shattering myths to reveal the largely forgotten, gripping truth behind the Gunpowder Plot. Robert Catesby, Thomas Wintour and Thomas Percy sought to ignite a Catholic rebellion by overthrowing King James and placing his young daughter Princess Elizabeth on the throne. Chuku Modu, Luke Hobson and Luke Roberts star

programme.title: David Attenboroughs Global Adventure
programme.desc: Documentary capturing some of the greatest filming sequences of David Attenboroughs career, using previously unseen footage, from the deepest dives in a submersible on the Great Barrier Reef to watching a million bats take off in a cave in Borneo. Other highlights include life in the frozen tundra of South Georgia, a colony of King Penguins and the worlds largest migration of Hammerhead sharks in the Galapagos Islands

programme.title: Great Barrier Reef with David Attenborough
programme.desc: Following his visit to the Great Barrier Reef in 1957, naturalist and broadcaster David Attenborough returns and uses the latest filming techniques to unlock the secrets of the natural wonder. In the first edition, he descends beneath the waves at night and uses cutting-edge technology to generate computer scans of the sea floor. He also learns about a complex community of animals, as well as the annual coral spawning, which is responsible for the great diversity in the reef

programme.title: Great Barrier Reef with David Attenborough
programme.desc: Using the latest technology, the naturalist explores the shark-infested waters of Osprey Reef in the Triton submersible, before returning to the nesting grounds of the Green Sea Turtle, 60 years after his first visit to Raine Island. He also marvels at manta ray cleaning stations on Lady Elliot Island, and tracks dwarf minke whales to discover their surprising reasons for returning, and why the reef is vital for their survival

programme.title: Great Barrier Reef with David Attenborough
programme.desc: The naturalist examines what the next few decades may hold for this remarkable ecosystem, and looks at the work of scientists who are trying to preserve the area and the species that inhabit it, assisted by research vessel the Alucia. He also embarks on an ambitious attempt to dive further into the reef than ever before to witness the resilient coral that live down in the depths and see if they hold answers that may help to overcome challenges that lie ahead, as the impact of humans on the reef continues to take its toll

programme.desc: Further footage of David Attenboroughs global wildlife adventures, from the depths of the Great Barrier Reef to the ancient rainforests of Borneo

programme.title: David Attenboroughs Rise of Animals
programme.sub-title: From the Seas to the Skies
programme.desc: Part one of two. The naturalist explores the evolution of vertebrates over the past 500 million years, beginning with a look at how remarkable advances allowed primitive fish to make the radical move on to land, and then take to the skies with the advent of flight. He uses fossils of both ancient and living species combined with CGI and film to chart their journey out of the water to populate all corners of the globe

programme.title: David Attenboroughs Rise of Animals
programme.desc: The naturalist concludes his two-part exploration of the evolution of vertebrates over the past 500 million years by looking at the history of mammals. He traces their journey from humble origins as tiny nocturnal forest-dwellers to their astonishing explosion in diversity and domination of the world following the death of the dinosaurs

programme.title: Agatha Christies Pale Horse
programme.desc: A sculptor discovers a dying priest who gives him a list of names. Mystery, starring Colin Buchanan, Jayne Ashbourne and Hermione Norris

programme.title: Animals Like Us
programme.desc: A look at the lives of animals, a penguin uses a small pebble to symbolise his proposal, while a lemur in Madagascar uses millipedes as a form of pest control

programme.title: Animals Like Us
programme.desc: A line of hermit crabs prepare for a very orderly house swap on a Belize beach. Plus, a chimpanzee that keeps a cat as a pet

programme.title: Planet Shark
programme.desc: Cameras venture underwater with a whitetip reef shark and a pelagic thresher shark as they hunt using heightened senses and killer instincts

programme.title: Planet Shark
programme.desc: Cameras follow nomadic sharks thriving in the oceans depths, a lone blue shark in the Atlantic and hundreds of hammerheads in the Pacific

programme.title: Planet Shark
programme.desc: The stunning underwater world of sharks, examining what makes these apex predators so successful and so deadly

programme.title: Leopard Dynasty: The Rise of Rana
programme.desc: Following a young leopard Prince Rana as he fights to secure his place in Jhalanas territory, even if it means facing off against his own father Bahadur

programme.title: Secret Life of the Tasmanian Devil
programme.desc: Part one of two. A look at the marsupial native only to the Australian island, revealing how the mating season is a chance for males to show who is dominant

programme.title: Secret Life of the Tasmanian Devil
programme.desc: Tasmanian Devils might have infamous reputations, but these spirited scrappers are committed parents and heroic defenders of their pouch-reared young

programme.title: David Attenboroughs Conquest of the Skies
programme.desc: The veteran naturalist examines the world of flight, travelling across the globe to chart the evolutionary story of those creatures who seemingly defy the rules of gravity by taking to the air. Initially focusing on insects, Attenborough meets a descendant of the dragonfly, before examining the Atlas beetle, which tends to restrict flight to times when it needs a mate or a fresh supply of food. More aesthetically pleasing is a group of beautiful tropical butterflies, followed by an in-depth study into the blow fly - the jet fighter of insects

programme.title: David Attenboroughs Conquest of the Skies
programme.desc: The veteran naturalist visits the jungles of Borneo to meet creatures that like to glide through life - the harlequin tree frog and the little-known colugo - often referred to, incorrectly, as a flying lemur. He also has a CGI-assisted encounter with the first true vertebrate flyer, the pterosaur, receives a flying lesson from a small flock of swans at Loch Lomond in Scotland and encounters the planets largest species of bat

programme.title: David Attenboroughs Conquest of the Skies
programme.desc: The veteran naturalist turns his attention to creatures that dominate the skies today. He begins with the vast bird family, watching a group of vultures as they go scavenging and some peregrine falcons dive bombing for their prey. He then moves on to the floating forests of Ecuador to find out what makes the humble hummingbird tick, before ending his journey at the Gomantong bat caves of Borneo - a place he first filmed in 1972 - witnessing a mass exodus of the creatures

programme.title: Deadly Australians
programme.desc: From the oceans and the forest to swamps and deserts, a look at the toxic, venomous and dangerous creatures that Australia is home to

programme.title: Big Cat Country
programme.desc: With the Hollywood lionesses hiding in the north, the four nomadic males turn south. Nathan Pilcher captures the Nomads attempt at a pride takeover, which sends the MK lions running for their lives

programme.title: Gladiators
programme.desc: Documentary revealing the secrets and ingenious tactics of natures toughest fighters in their battles for survival. Narrated by Sean Bean

programme.title: Gladiators
programme.desc: The secrets and ingenious tactics of Australias toughest fighters, from a kangaroos deadly boxing moves to the redback spiders precision-engineered trap

programme.title: Africas Wild Roommates: How Animals Share Bed and Board
programme.desc: A look at how when weaver birds, aardvarks and termites build homes in the African savannah, they find other animals moving in - creatures that all contribute something to the home

programme.title: Big Cat Country
programme.desc: With the Hollywood lionesses hiding in the north, the four nomadic males turn south. Nathan Pilcher captures the Nomads attempt at a pride takeover, which sends the MK lions running for their lives

programme.title: Attenborough and the Giant Elephant
programme.desc: Naturalist David Attenborough investigates the life of Jumbo, a famous Victorian elephant, supposedly the largest that ever lived. Jumbo arrived at London Zoo in 1865 and was popular with the royal family, but was dangerously unhappy in captivity. In later life, he would be sold to PT Barnums circus, becoming a celebrity before dying under mysterious circumstances. Attenborough examines Jumbos remains and considers the lives of elephants in the wild to learn more about the animals difficult life

programme.title: Bears of the Karawank Mountains
